such as TCP, multicast, would be highly regarded * Hands\-on experience with CI/CD orchestration tools * Exposure to deployment orchestration platforms (e.g. Octopus Deploy) * Proficiency with enterprise version control platforms (e.g. GitLab) * Understanding and practical application of security models (e.g., RBAC) * B.S. in a technical discipline or equivalent experience. * Financial trade floor experience is a plus but not essential, training will be provided **Personal Skills** * Initiative – proven record of seeking continuous improvement and implementing best practice in technology environment * Ability to work under pressure and tight deadlines * Self\-motivated and creative * Strong communication (verbal and written) – ability to convey technically complex subjects in a clear and concise manner * Attention to detail – thorough in work carried out * Interpersonal – friendly and approachable * Teamwork – working together to meet deadlines * Excellent time management, organization and prioritization skills * Flexibility – all members of the team participate in early morning and out of hours support schedules * Desire to learn and improve skills and knowledge Susquehanna is an equal opportunity employer. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application or recruitment process, please contact us. *If you're a recruiting agency and want to partner with us, please reach out to recruiting@sig.com. Any resume or referral submitted in the absence of a signed agreement will not be eligible for an agency fee.*

title: EMEA Digital Workspace Engineer
description: **Requirements** **Description and Requirements** * we will consider applications from European locations where MetLife has a presence. Warsaw, Poland would be the preference. Our company is currently undergoing a significant transformation across the EMEA region. We operate six regional data centers that play a key role in supporting our test environments. Historically, each region has managed its own processes and infrastructure independently. However, we are now moving toward a more unified, global operating model. The goal of this transformation is to strengthen our internal knowledge base and improve collaboration between regional and global teams. By consolidating expertise and standardizing our practices, we aim to increase efficiency and reduce duplication of effort. This shift will also allow us to provide more consistent and scalable support worldwide. This transformation also focuses on improving collaboration with our internal business partners and responding more effectively to their needs. **The Team You Will Join** The Digital Workspace team, responsible for the desktop experience for users across the region, is part of an infrastructure team focused on transforming and supporting the technology platforms providing critical services to our businesses in around 20 countries across EMEA. **The Opportunity** In this critical role as an EMEA Digital Workspace Engineer, you will be responsible for ensuring the health and performance of all end\-user devices across EMEA, as well as the effective delivery of services and applications to the same. The ideal candidate has strong technical expertise in modern endpoint management tools and a passion for delivering an exceptional technology experience for our colleagues. You will be part of an extended global team and will collaborate with global functions on a daily basis. As an extended team with responsibility for Desktop Experience, Desktop Services, Desktop Application Services and PC Lifecycle you will coordinate and collaborate with these teams to ensure effective delivery of services to our region. **How You’ll Help Us Build a Confident Future (Key Responsibilities)** * Utilise advanced tools like Nexthink, and Microsoft Intune to monitor, maintain, and report on the performance and compliance of thousands of end\-user devices. * Develop and produce effective regular reporting, help define and monitor KPIs and performance trends. * Collaborate closely with global teams to manage endpoint builds, laptop currency and refresh cycles, and the seamless implementation of new user\-facing tools and features. * Champion the adoption and optimisation of new technologies, including AI tools like Microsoft Copilot for the desktop, to enhance user productivity. * Perform in\-depth issue identification and trend analysis on endpoint performance data to proactively identify problems and drive optimisation efforts. * Serve as a key technical contact point for desktop technology services within the EMEA region. **What You Need To Succeed (Required Qualifications)** * 2\+ years of experience in a similar digital workspace, desktop engineering, or endpoint management role. * Proven experience working and collaborating effectively within an extended technical team. * Good level of proficiency with modern endpoint management and monitoring tools, specifically Nexthink, Intune and Ivanti. * Good awareness of Microsoft endpoint management solutions generally (SCCM, Autopilot etc). * Excellent analytical, problem\-solving, and communication skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively with global teams. * Fluent and effective at communicating in English. * Bachelor’s degree in information technology, computer science, or equivalent practical experience. **What Can Give You An Edge (Additional Skills)** * Experience working within a large, global financial services or insurance company. * Hands\-on experience with the creation of Power\-BI dashboards to display key historical and future indicator information related to packaging, OS, app deployment and currency saturation. * Relevant industry certifications (e.g., Microsoft 365 Certified: Endpoint Administrator Associate, ITIL Foundation, Nexthink or other relevant certifications). * Experience scripting with PowerShell for automation and reporting. **Benefits We Offer** Our Ireland benefits address holistic well\-being with programs for physical and mental health, financial wellness, and support for families. We are committed to employee care by delivering comprehensive inclusive offerings and cultivating an environment that drives professional growth, purposeful work, and a supportive culture. Our benefits package includes hybrid work, career development and professional qualification support, full private health insurance for employees and their immediate family members, income protection, defined contribution pension benefits and much more! **About MetLife** Recognized on Fortune magazine's list of the "World's Most Admired Companies" and Fortune World’s 25 Best Workplaces™, MetLife, through its subsidiaries and affiliates, is one of the world’s leading financial services companies; providing insurance, annuities, employee benefits and asset management to individual and institutional customers. With operations in more than 40 markets, we hold leading positions in the United States, Latin America, Asia, Europe, and the Middle East. Our purpose is simple \- to help our colleagues, customers, communities, and the world at large create a more confident future. United by purpose and guided by our core values \- Win Together, Do the Right Thing, Deliver Impact Over Activity, and Think Ahead \- we’re inspired to transform the next century in financial services. At MetLife, it’s \#AllTogetherPossible . Join us!

title: EMR Technician II
description: **Join Us** **Build the Backbone of Reliable Asset Data as an EMR Technician II — Support Precision, Strengthen Systems, Drive Operational Insight** Are you detail\-driven and energized by hands\-on fieldwork that directly improves asset accuracy and maintenance performance? This is an excellent opportunity for a technically curious professional who values accuracy, teamwork, and continuous learning while contributing to high\-impact asset management projects. **What members * Assist with inventory coordination, including movement and organization of equipment within stockroom environments as well as vendor coordination for recommended spare parts and equipment To support these values, we invite all qualified applicants to be considered for employment regardless of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, citizenship, age, disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.**

title: Educational Psychologist at the National Educational Psychological Service
description: **Job title:** Educational Psychologist at the National Educational Psychological Service **Employer:** Department of Education and Youth **Location:** Louth **Closing Date:** 3pm, Thursday, 11th June 2026 **Salary:** Starting at €65,783 **About the Employer:** The mission of the Department of Education and Youth is to ensure that all children and young people have access to a positive learning experience and to facilitate them, through learning, to realise their full potential and contribute to Ireland’s social, economic and cultural development. The Department’s goals are to enable the provision of high\-quality education and improve the learning experience to meet the needs of all children and young people in schools and early learning and care settings; to ensure equity of opportunity in education and that all children and young people are supported to fulfil their potential; together with partners, to provide strategic leadership and support for the delivery of the right systems and infrastructure for the sector; and to ensure organisational excellence and innovation. The National Educational Psychological Service (NEPS) is the psychological service of the Department of Education and Youth. As a section of the Department, NEPS provides a comprehensive, school\-based educational psychological service to all primary, post primary schools and special schools. NEPS is led by a National Director, Assistant Directors and local Senior Team Leaders. Through the application of psychological theory and practice, NEPS psychologists support the wellbeing, and the academic, social and emotional development of all learners. NEPS has particular regard for children with special educational needs (SEN) and those at risk of marginalisation due to educational disadvantage. The area of supporting wellbeing/mental health in schools is a rapidly expanding area of focus for NEPS. **About the Role:** NEPS’ Educational Psychologists are assigned to a local team of psychologists coordinated by a Senior Team Leading Psychologist. NEPS psychologists provide an educational psychological service for their assigned schools, generally in the catchment area of their assigned office, and may also work collaboratively with colleagues to meet NEPS national service priorities, and regional and team commitments. Within this context, each psychologist takes responsibility for their own day\-to\-day time and work management. The Principal Duties and Responsibilities of the Role may include some or all of the following: * Ensuring the delivery of a high quality professional educational psychological service to their assigned schools, and to other schools, as appropriate, in accordance with the Regional Service Plan, the Business Plan of NEPS and the Statement of Strategy of the Department of Education and Youth. * Supporting national and regional Support and Development initiatives to build capacity/capability in assigned schools and more broadly in schools within the region. * Developing co\-operative linkages with other educational services in the local team area and region. * Developing co\-operative linkages with other relevant statutory and voluntary bodies in the local team area region. * Attending regular supervision as a core professional activity to support the delivery of an effective, ethical and high\-quality educational psychological service. * Contributing to the development of the NEPS service through participation at team, regional and national meetings and participation in working groups. * Adhering to the Code of Professional Ethics of the Psychological Society of Ireland (2019\), whether a member of that society or not. Under the Health \& Social Care Professionals Act 2005 all psychologists working in NEPS will have to fulfil the requirements for statutory registration. * Fulfilling their functions in line with the policies, protocols and legislative obligations of the Department. * Engaging with continuing professional development (CPD), including a comprehensive induction programme and ongoing opportunities for national, regional and personal continuing professional development. NEPS psychologists are given the opportunity to review their development needs through the Department’s Performance Management and Development System (PMDS). * Supporting the training placements of Trainee Educational Psychologists in NEPS through the provision of professional supervision and guidance. **About You:** Candidates must have on or before 11th of June 2026: 1\.A recognised qualification at minimum Level 8 (1st or 2nd class honours) on the National Framework of Qualifications (NFQ) or a NARIC Ireland Foreign Qualifications equivalent in Psychology1\. Candidates’ Psychology qualification(s) should deem them eligible to hold graduate membership of the Psychological Society of Ireland (PSI) or the British Psychological Society (BPS). (Evidence of graduate membership/eligibility to hold graduate membership will be requested from candidates should they come under consideration for a position) **AND** 2\.(a) A post\-graduate professional qualification in educational psychology, that includes supervised practice placement in an educational psychological service working with school\- aged children and young people, accredited\* by the PSI or equivalent body2\. Students currently undertaking such an accredited post\-graduate professional qualification in educational psychology who expect to successfully complete their course of study in 20263, and who meet criterion 1 above will also be considered. **OR** (b) A post\-graduate professional qualification in clinical or counselling psychology, accredited\* by the PSI, or equivalent body, and at least two years continuous supervised post\-graduate professional experience employed as a psychologist working with children and young people in education, community or disability settings. Candidates must also have: 3\.Experience of engaging in individual casework with children and young people, including experience using consultation skills within a continuum of support framework; administering, interpreting and reporting on a range of psychometric instruments suitable for use with children and young people; formulating multiple data to identify need linked to specific and targeted interventions. 4\.A comprehensive knowledge and understanding of the Irish Education and school systems, experience working systemically to promote change within an education system, commitment to the ideals and objectives of NEPS/Department of Education and Youth and willingness to work within the overall parameters of NEPS/ Department of Education and Youth policy and practice. 5\.The ability to communicate effectively in Irish and/or English; 6\.A full driving licence (Category B), valid in Ireland, and have access to a suitable vehicle that will enable them to carry out the full mobility requirements of the post in an efficient and effective manner. Further information including details on how to apply for this position is available on www.publicjobs.ie. The closing date for receipt of completed applications is 3pm on Thursday, 11th June, 2026\. We are committed to a policy of equal opportunity and encourage applications under all nine grounds of the Employment Equality Act.

title: Electrical Maintenance Technician
description: **Background:** Veolia is Ireland’s leading environmental services company. We provide a comprehensive range of energy, waste and water solutions and are dedicated to carbon reduction, protecting the environment and building the circular economy. We offer all employees a benefits package as standard which includes full VHI cover, pension and education assistance. When you see the world as we do, you see the chance to help the world take better care of its resources, and help it become a better place for everyone. It's why we're looking for someone who's just as committed as we are, to push for genuine change and bring our ambition of Ecological Transformation to life. We know that everyone here at Veolia can help us work alongside our communities, look after the environment, and contribute to our inclusive culture. **Electrical Maintenance Technician** **Location: Blanchardstown** **Hybrid/Onsite: Onsite** **Working Pattern (Time/Hours): 39 Hours per week (07:30 to 16:00\)** **Duration: Permanent** **Overview of the role:** The Utilities Maintenance Technician is responsible for the execution of the preventive and corrective maintenance program as per client requirements in a safe, efficient and competent manner in line with cGMP and site protocols and procedures. He/she will work closely with day and shift colleagues and leads to ensure all work is carried out safely, correctly and as efficiently as possible. **Duties of the Role include;** * All work on site to be carried out in accordance with site EHS (Environmental Health and Safety) and Veolia EHS and cGMP rules and guidelines; any irregularities to be reported to the Maintenance \& Calibration Lead or Contract Manager. * Carry out work as per schedule supplied by the Maintenance \& Calibrations Lead, Planner and as per site policies, schedules and procedures. * Troubleshoot problems with utilities equipment as necessary and as directed by site supervisors * Complete site documentation before and after work tasks, as appropriate * Liaise with Contractors during planned service maintenance. * Participate in the appropriate training. * Carry out Risk Assessment of Equipment Maintenance Task List steps and ensure these are always kept valid. * Carry out Periodic reviews of WI’s/SOP to ensure documents are always current and valid. * Flexibility to work on equipment in all buildings * Work and interact with the client utilities engineering team * Flexibility to work around maintenance shutdown’s * Be familiar with all GMP rules and regulations * Be familiar with statutory requirements * Be responsible for his/her training curricula and ensure missing requirements are completed * Maintenance of housekeeping standards compatible with pharmaceutical manufacturing in the general plant, control rooms and compatibility with clean room requirements as required. Responsible for applying 5 S methodology in all areas. * Implement and participate in utilities reliability programs * If/when required, provide technical input/ support during the implementation of new projects from design through to beneficial use. **Required skills for this role;** * Senior Trades, City \& Guilds or equivalent in electrical engineering. * Electrical experience, 2 years minimum and good understanding of the basic fundamentals of the various utilities systems. ***Essential:*** * Experience on the following: + Pumps, HVAC Systems, Compressors, Boilers, steam systems and ancillary services, (DHW skids), Chillers, Cooling Towers, Clean steam Boilers, Purified Water Systems, CIP systems, Low /Medium Voltage Electrical Systems. * Good understanding of Preventive Maintenance Systems. * Work within a Computerized Maintenance Management Work Order system environment. * Work within a documented Permit to Work System * Computer literate * Ability to read and understand P\&ID’s. * Understanding of SOP’s and GMP Engineering systems. * Understanding of Basic Instrumentation * Must be able to demonstrate hands on experience with detailed knowledge/skill in the following area’s: * Team player. * Attention to detail * Methodical \& analytical approach to work. * Good problem solving and trouble shooting ability. * Good Initiative and pro\-active attitude in approach to duties ***Desirable:*** * Site Level 1 Electrical Qualification * Building Management Systems (Scada) * Experience of waste water treatment plants; * PLC’s * Troubleshooting / repair of mechanical systems **Please note:** If you are an EU/EEA national, you will be asked to show proof of right to work in Ireland. If you are a non\-EU/EEA national , you will require current and valid permission to work and reside in the Republic of Ireland. Information on legislation and guides to the procedures in relation to obtaining greencard permits ,work permits, spousal/dependant permits is available on the Department of Jobs, Enterprise and Innovation website.

title: Engineer, Quality
description: **Job Overview** As an Engineer, QA Automation at iCIMS, you will play a critical role in ensuring the delivery of high\-quality, reliable, and scalable software solutions across iCIMS. You will be responsible for designing and executing automated and manual test plans, identifying and documenting defects, and collaborating closely with engineers, product owners, and DevOps engineers to embed quality throughout the software development lifecycle. Off hours support as needed. This is a hybrid position based at Windmill Lane, Dublin 2, our strategic hub for AI development in Ireland. **About Us** When you join iCIMS, you join the team helping global companies transform business and the world through the power of talent. Our customers do amazing things: design rocket ships, create vaccines, deliver consumer goods globally, overnight, with a smile. As the Talent Cloud company, we empower these organizations to attract, engage, hire, and advance the right talent. We’re passionate about helping companies build a diverse, winning workforce and about building our home team. We're dedicated to fostering an inclusive, purpose\-driven, and innovative work environment where everyone belongs. **Responsibilities** **In Sprint Testing:** * Design and implement test plans as part of in\-sprint testing team * Build and maintain E2E and Integration Tests prior to production release * Manually test all features as they become available and provide timely feedback on readiness * Assist in code reviews and code/architecture quality discussions * Execute E2E performance scripts as needed * Create, document, and troubleshoot defects * Provide “Go/No\-Go” decision on release quality for applications under development * Perform post deployment checkout for features developed in sprint **Develop and Maintain Automation Scripts:** * Review current automation framework and make improvements * Create and manage the code repositories for multiple test suites using multiple automation tools * Ensure automation scripts meet acceptable reliability, performance, and maintenance requirements * Perform code reviews for other engineers **Act as the “Voice of Quality”:** * Review customer feedback, incidents, and test results from alpha/beta tests to inform test title: Engineers – Engineering, Asset Management & Offshore
description: We are at the heart of Ireland’s energy transition \- connecting, operating and transforming the electricity grid to support a low carbon, renewable future. We are building talented teams that tackle real world challenges every day and help us to achieve our goal to prepare the grid to carry up to 80% of Ireland’s electricity from renewable sources. We are proud to be a certified Great Place to Work, recognised for our culture built on trust, collaboration and inclusion. At EirGrid you will find supportive teams, opportunities to grow and meaningful work. We also offer secure employment with a range of flexible and family friendly working policies to support your life and wellbeing. If you want to know more about what EirGrid has to offer you, visit Life at EirGrid. If you are ready to make an impact and grow your career in a purpose\-led organisation, your journey starts here. **The Opportunity** We now seeking Engineers to join our Engineering \& Asset Management (E\&AM). We have variety of new roles from junior engineers to senior lead engineers for the following areas: Client Engineering, Asset Management and Offshore. The successful candidates will perform engineering assignments to ensure that business priorities are met. The client engineering function is responsible for undertaking project design reviews/site inspections on both contestable and non\-contestable transmission projects to ensure that projects are developed in accordance with EirGrid functional specification standards. Delivery of these projects is required to meet key departmental and company objectives. **The Team** The Engineering \& Asset Management is responsible for undertaking four key roles; asset management, technical authority, onshore engineering and offshore engineering. The team is responsible for setting the technical engineering policies and standards to be used for transmission infrastructure, monitoring compliance during design and construction and for ensuring asset integrity and ensuring uninterrupted power system functionality. The Engineering and Asset Management and Offshore Delivery are the areas within EirGrid which facilitates that role through the development of technical standards and functional specifications for all plant to be connected to the transmission system and the development of new and innovative technologies and methodologies. In addition, these areas are responsible for ensuring that EirGrid’s requirements are incorporated in project design and construction whether the works are carried out by ESB Networks or by third parties. **About you** Successful candidates will have:* Electrical Engineering qualification to degree level or equivalent qualification, typically with 4\+ years relevant post\-qualification experience. * Good technical knowledge in high voltage transmission design with strong knowledge of transmission technology including new and emerging technologies. * Transmission Substation design expertise with experience assessing substation designs for conformity to standards at 110/220/400 kV. * Excellent communication skills with an emphasis on high quality report writing and ability. * Experience preparing and issuing transmission design drawings, such as single line diagrams, substation layout drawings, protection schematics and interlocking drawings, etc * Experience completing onsite inspections and monitoring of construction progress for compliance. * Full drivers licence. **Closing date for applications** 5th June 2026 *Please note we receive a high volume of applications, and as every application is carefully reviewed our response time may vary. If you agree to be considered for other roles this may result in a delayed response as we establish the best fit for your experience. We appreciate your patience.* We value potential as much as experience. If you believe you can succeed in this role, we encourage you to apply, even if you don’t meet every requirement. We're dedicated to diversity and inclusion at EirGrid. We recognise the strength that comes from having a diverse workforce and the importance of a supportive culture for all our people to achieve their potential. Our diversity and inclusion networks have been designed to create an inclusive culture that enables our people to feel a sense of belonging at work. As an equal opportunities’ employer, we welcome applications from people of all backgrounds. Reasonable accommodations are offered at every stage of our recruitment process.

title: Field Service Engineer
description: Schneider Electric is seeking a skilled and motivated Field Service Engineer to join our ASCO Power Services team in Ireland. As a global leader in energy management and automation, we deliver innovative and reliable power solutions to critical infrastructure and industrial customers. In this role, you will support customers across Ireland by installing, maintaining, and troubleshooting electrical power systems and equipment. You will play a key part in ensuring system reliability, safety, and customer satisfaction while working as part of a highly experienced field services team. Key Responsibilities * Carry out on\-site inspection, commissioning, and fault\-finding of ASCO and related power systems (e.g. ATS, switchgear, Power Control Systems). * Perform planned preventative maintenance (PPM) and reactive service work to maintain optimal system performance. * Diagnose and resolve faults efficiently, providing clear technical recommendations to customers. * Maintain accurate service reports, documentation, and compliance records in line with company and regulatory standards. * Build strong relationships with customers through professional communication and high service standards. * Ensure all work is carried out in compliance with Irish health \& safety regulations and Schneider Electric policies. * Participate in on\-call / standby rota where required. * Collaborate with internal teams (sales, engineering, operations) to support project delivery and identify service opportunities. * Contribute to continuous improvement and knowledge sharing within the team. Location \& Travel * Based in Ireland (ideally Dublin / Leinster region), with regular travel nationwide. * Occasional travel to the UK or Europe may be required. **Qualifications \& Experience** * Technical qualification in Electrical / Electronic Engineering (Level 6 or higher preferred). * Strong experience in power systems, switchgear, or critical power equipment. * Proven ability in fault diagnosis, repair, and maintenance of electrical systems. * Understanding of low voltage / medium voltage systems (desirable). * Full, clean Irish driving licence required. Key Skills * Strong technical troubleshooting and analytical skills * Excellent communication and customer\-facing ability * Good time management and organisational skills * Ability to work independently and as part of a team * Commitment to health, safety, and quality standards What We Offer * Competitive salary with overtime and on\-call allowances * Company vehicle and tools * Competitive salary \& Bonus Scheme, * 28 Days Annual Leave \+ Public Holidays, * Private Health Care, * Pension Scheme and Life Assurance * Worldwide Employee Share Ownership Programme, * Various Health \& Wellbeing Support Options, Employee Assistance Programme. * Cycle to Work Scheme * CareerHub (internal mobility opportunities \+ access to mentors \& projects globally), * Learning Portal and much more. As a result, our teams are stronger to drive the company's future. We are proudly participating in the Disability Confident Program offering support to people in the workplace who consider themselves living with a disability. Apply now: Please submit an online application to be considered for any position with us. You know about us, so let us learn about you! title: FinOps Engineer - 12 Month FTC
description: **Sustainability that means business** **Who We Are** Sustainability software specialist, AMCS, is headquartered in Ireland, with offices in Europe, the USA, and Australasia. With over 1,300 highly\-skilled employees across 22 countries, we specialize in delivering technology solutions to facilitate a carbon neutral future. **What We Do** Our innovative SaaS solutions increase efficiency and boost sustainability in resource\-intensive industries. Over 5,000 customers across 23 countries already benefit from our Performance Sustainability software, ensuring we deliver practical solutions for improved profitability and environmental resilience across the globe. **Our People** AMCS offers team members more than just a job, but an opportunity to map out a career with a company that is growing, evolving and setting out new ways of working that are having a positive impact on the world around us. AMCS was established in Ireland and holds onto those local roots and ‘start\-up’ mentality with a culture of connection. Connection to our work, our customers, our colleagues and our community that creates a working environment that fosters openness, collaboration and creativity. **Role Purpose** The FinOps Engineer will drive financial accountability, cost optimization and operational efficiency across cloud platforms within the Cloud Operations organization. This role ensures visibility into cloud spend, implements cost\-effective provisioning strategies, and balances financial efficiency with engineering agility. **Key Responsibilities** * Develop and execute a comprehensive FinOps roadmap aligned with organizational goals. * Establish governance policies for resource utilization, provisioning, and budget approvals. * Financial Accountability * Collaborate with Finance, Product, and Engineering teams to ensure cost transparency and budgeting discipline. * Manage vendor relationships (cloud providers, software suppliers), raise purchase orders, and ensure timely invoice processing. * Operational Excellence * Design and implement a FinOps observability platform to monitor cloud costs and usage. * Integrate cloud cost management tools into the SDLC. * Define and track KPIs for cloud cost optimization and efficiency. * Implement policies for instance rightsizing, resource provisioning, and decommissioning unused resources. * Drive automation initiatives for cost efficiency across cloud environments. * Education \& Awareness * Promote FinOps best practices across engineering teams. * Conduct training sessions to improve cost awareness and financial literacy within technical teams. **Required Skills \& Experience** * Proven experience in FinOps, Cloud Operations, or Cloud Financial Management. * Strong understanding of cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) and associated cost models. * Experience with cost management tools. * Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills. * Ability to design governance frameworks and implement automation for cost optimization. **Preferred Qualifications** * FinOps Certified Practitioner or similar certification. * Background in DevOps, SRE, or Cloud Architecture. * Familiarity with budgeting and financial processes in large organizations

title: Finance Business Partner
description: **About Ethos** Ethos is a Greek word meaning "character". It is used to describe the guiding beliefs or ideals that characterise a community, nation, or ideology. Ethos are mechanical and electrical consultants to the data centre, commercial, high\-tech healthcare, and pharmaceutical sectors. Our mission is to engineer systems that complement the purpose of each building we design, improve the immediate and surrounding community, and meet the objectives of its operations, inhabitants, patrons, operators, and staff. We are an ambitious, innovative, and driven. We are passionate about delivering excellence for our clients and their projects, and we never settle for delivering work quickly at the cost of quality. Our work environment is positive, professional, and supportive. We take our work seriously, but we like to have a lot of fun too and we place huge importance on having a great company culture. We have been recognised as a Great Place to Work in 2019\-2020 and in 2020\-2021 and were awarded Best Company to Work For 2022\. **About the role:** Ethos prides itself on delivering efficient, effective solutions across a wide range of industries. We’re currently searching for a qualified accountant to join our ranks as a Finance Business Partner and continue our tradition of success. This person will play an important role across all areas of finance for the entity, as well as supporting the FP\&A and Operations teams and other Ad hoc tasks as necessary. **Role Specification** **Candidate Attributes** * The candidate must be based in Ireland to be consider for this position * 2\-3 years post qualified experience (PQE) essential * Ideally working in an industry role in construction or service provider * Experience working in the UK market desirable but not essential * Strong commercial acumen * Good organisational skills, able to prioritise effectively \& escalate as necessary * Proven track record of strong work ethic. Clear examples of “rolling up sleeves and getting stuck in” * Proven track record of problem solving with clear examples * Proactive \& effective communicator, able to communicate both internally within the business and externally to clients \& customers * Team player, ability to work both independently and collaboratively with other team members, in pursuit of finance objectives * Strong IT skills, including a proficiency in the use of excel. Experience working with financial systems / ERP a must * Continuous improvement mindset **Your Day\-to\-Day Activities:** * Own the role as finance business partner and point of contact for all finance related matters for Ethos’ UK based subsidiary. You will be required to present analysis, provide insight and make recommendations for future strategies * Assist commercial team with pricing, invoicing and cash collection * Own the accounts payable process implementing a purchase order process and keeping creditor balances within terms * Own cash flow management for the company reporting to the financial controller and managing director of the business on a weekly and monthly basis * Own the payroll process working with HR and external payroll provider to ensure accurate processing and payment of payroll on monthly basis * Take ownership of the company’s ERP system maintaining accurate master data and ensuring adequate training and resourcing is provided to the rest of the team * Play an active role in month end including: * Production of company management accounts within group deadlines * Ensure all areas are prepared to a high degree of accuracy * Work with the Financial Controller and other finance team members on timely delivery of reporting deliverables * Manage general ledger postings, reconciliations and journal entries * Work with local compliance partners of foreign subsidiaries around tax and statutory filings. * Take ownership of balance sheet reconciliations and ensure all completed accurately and on time * Support the annual audit process, liaising with external auditors and preparing necessary schedules. * Maintain and improve internal controls, ensuring adherence to company policies * Assist with budgeting, forecasting, and variance analysis. * Provide financial analysis and reporting to support strategic and operational decisions. * Play an active role in new ERP system implementation * Occasional travel to the UK will be required * Assist financial controller and financial accountants with group reporting requirements * Assist Financial Controller and CFO with any ad hoc tasks. **Why Join us?** At Ethos, we’re dedicated to fostering an environment that encourages both **technical excellence and personal wellbeing**. **We offer:** * **Flexible Fridays** and a **9\-day fortnight working schedule** — enjoy every second Friday off to maintain work–life balance. * A **state\-of\-the\-art office in Charlemont, Dublin 2**, designed to promote collaboration, innovation, and wellbeing. * A culture that values **flexibility, learning, and teamwork**, with clear progression pathways and support toward **professional accreditation**. **Benefits:** * Flexible working times (in agreement with your Direct Manager) * Pension Scheme * Travel tax scheme or bike\-to\-work scheme * Regular training/mentoring / further college support * Subsidised Health Insurance Allowance * A Professional Subscription Fee paid by the company * Paid Statutory Leaves * Healthcare check\-ups and flu vaccinations * Gym / Fitness membership * Heavily subsidised social committee (5 a side, golf society, climbing club and regular social events) Here at Ethos we believe in equal opportunities and that each individual can bring something ‘WoW’ to our organisation. So if you don't necessarily meet every single point on the job description, but think you have what it takes, please still get in touch. We'd love to have a chat and see if you could be a great fit. Our vision is to: “**Be the company people want to work with, and for**” Our values differentiate and focus us every day to be EPIC \- for our people and our customers.

title: Finance Business Partner
description: **Finance Business Partner \- Ireland, Dublin \- 12 Month Contract** Our client is a global leader in the pharmaceutical industry. Due to continued expansion, significant capital project investment, and ongoing growth across their manufacturing portfolio, they are now seeking an experienced Finance Business Partner to join their state\-of\-the\-art facility near Dublin. This is an excellent opportunity to contribute to large\-scale pharmaceutical manufacturing projects within a world\-class organisation. **What You’ll Do** * Act as a support for TPIB and other Complex Entities in the S4Hana program * Site level and complex entity level coordination and engagement on S4Hana upgrade programme * Support S4Hana data validations, process validation and migrations * Post implementation issue resolution and support **What you’ll need:** * ACA/ACCA qualification * Strong technical accounting \& financial reporting knowledge * Prior experience with SAP * Excellent IT skills including BI reporting an advantage * Detail oriented, flexible with can do attitude. * Strong written and verbal communication skills **Apply now to make an impact to the pharmaceutical industry!**

title: Finance Treasury Specialist ( fixed term contract )
description: Currencyfair is a global fintech. We make cross border payments for our customers. This role is in the heart of what we do. Strong reconciliation experience is a must and a passion for working alongside your team mates to make our business run smoothly and safely. We are seeking candidates for a fixed term contract 6 \- 9 months possibly extendable. **Role Description** As the Finance Treasury Analyst you will play a crucial role in managing and optimising the treasury function within our FX business based in Ireland and ensuring the correct reconciliation and reporting of our safeguarded accounts. You will be responsible for, and supporting various financial initiatives. The role requires a strong understanding of financial cash flows, foreign exchange transactions, treasury management, and a proactive approach to driving efficiency and effectiveness in treasury operations. **Key Responsibilities** **Safeguarding account management:** * Ensure completeness and filing of daily safeguarding accounts process. * Ensue 100% of all daily reconciliations are completed and reviewed * Alignment and partnership with Operations Treasury and Compliance teams. * Investigative, analysis and oversee resolution of breaks. * Maintain Safeguarding Policy and Procedures are in line with current processes and followed * Manage any breach escalation incident in line with policy * Reporting of the firms’ funds, operating and client accounts. * Ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and internal policies related to foreign exchange operations. **Treasury Management** **Banking Relationships:** * Manage relationships with banking partners, including foreign exchange service providers and correspondent banks, acting as the main point of contact for inquiries and service needs. Negotiate competitive foreign exchange rates and transaction fees. * Coordinate with banks for cash management services, account maintenance, and other treasury\-related matters. **Financial Risk Management** * Identify and analyse financial risks, including foreign exchange risk, interest rate risk, and credit risk. * Develop risk mitigation strategies, such as hedging programs and derivative instruments. * Monitor compliance with risk management policies and procedures. **Treasury Operations** * Oversee treasury operations, including cash receipts and disbursements, wire transfers, and bank reconciliations. * Ensure accurate and timely execution of transactions while adhering to internal controls and regulatory requirements. * Implement and enhance treasury systems and processes to improve efficiency and scalability. **Reporting And Analysis** * Analyse financial data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities for optimisation. * Support financial planning and budgeting processes by providing treasury\-related insights and recommendations. * Prepare regular reports on bank costs, hedging costs, interest, revenue and operational expenses * Prepare Safeguarding reporting to ERC

title: Financial Accountant
description: Financial Accountant, Mercury Headquarters, Ballycoolin, Dublin Apply Now **Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions.** We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities, and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres, Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Scope Of The Role** The right candidate will support our journey to streamline financial and operational processes, including implementation of new systems \& processes to accommodate rapid growth and have outstanding communication and interpersonal skills. You must be a driven, high energy, customer service focused individual that can work well with internal and external stakeholders at all levels, a critical thinker who can drive projects and make an impact. You have exceptional collaboration and communication skills and a desire to help drive business goals in a matrix organization. You are a process improver that can easily pick up the high\-level business goals and apply them to the finance and accounting operations. **Key Responsibilities Of The Role** * Reporting to the GL Leads * General Ledger team member duties + Month\-end \& Year\-end closure of accounts + Ensure data entered and postings made are on\-time and accurate across all key areas such as revenue, costs, FX, prepayments, accruals, and all areas covering the P\&L and Balance Sheet + Detailed review before, during and after each step is completed to ensure the highest level of quality. + Ensure policies and procedures are followed and that all control steps are completed. * Supporting elements of the year\-end audit both at group and local levels as well as internal audit queries * Support the Identification and delivery of solutions that will enhance and automate existing processes. Challenge the current approach, offer views on improvement, support implementation of those improvements. * Support the on\-going work to develop and document business processes and accounting policies to maintain and strengthen internal controls. * Create strong business partnerships within your own team and across all teams in the business. Be a team player, success in this role is based on working with others throughout the business. * Key callout, Mercury is on a journey to strengthen its financial reporting processes, controls, and team structure, this is a key focus. **Essential Criteria For The Role** * Qualified accountant (ACA/ACCA or equivalent) with a minimum 3 years’ experience in multinational environment. * Experience in using SAP and managing month\-end \& other core General Ledger related activities is essential. * Relevant industry PQE with large, fast\-growing, multi\-national company. * Very organised and thorough with desire for continuous improvement, attention to detail * Strong foundation in Financial Accounting compliance with experience of initiating and driving projects to completion. * Proven ability to multitask, with ability to design plans and deliver against them. * Strong IT and analytical skills * Experienced in Microsoft Suite with strong skills in Excel (SAP BW and PowerBi experience would also be useful) * Excellent verbal, written, and interpersonal skills. * Self\-starter with a drive to deliver high quality work, on\-time while adding value. ***Mercury is an Equal Opportunity Employer.*** Apply Now

title: Financial Crime Investigator - FTC - 12 months
description: Who we are: TrueLayer is Europe’s fastest\-growing Pay by Bank network. We power smarter, safer and faster payments by combining real\-time bank payments with financial and identity data. E\-commerce, iGaming and Financial Services businesses use our products to onboard new users, accept money and make payouts in seconds, and at scale. Live across 22 countries and with 1 new user joining TrueLayer’s Pay by Bank network every 3 seconds, we’re in a very exciting growth stage. This is why we’re expanding the team across our offices in London, Milan, and Dublin. Listed in Sifted as one of the 100 fastest growing start\-ups in UK \& Ireland, and CNBC’s World Top Fintech Companies, we’re trusted by industry leaders like RyanAir and Just Eat Takeaway for continued innovation within our payment products. To date, we’ve raised over $300 million from world\-renowned investors including Stripe, Tiger Global, Northzone, and Tencent. But this is just the beginning. We’re looking for people who are excited about joining the Pay by Bank revolution and helping us change the way the world pays. Could this be you? **Description** **This is a fixed\-term contract for 12 months.** The TrueLayer Financial Crime Investigations team has several key responsibilities designed to identify and report on suspected serious predicate financial crime offences that may be occurring within the Truelayer customer transactional ecosystem. This includes the day\-to\-day investigation of Transaction Monitoring alerts and sanctions screening alerts. This is an exciting opportunity for a FinCrime Investigator to join Truelayer and support these ongoing monitoring processes. The FinCrime Investigator will play an integral role in maintaining our first line of defence against financial crime, including money laundering, terrorist financing, fraud and other serious financial crime offences. This role sits within the Investigations team within the Financial Crime Department, which is part of the wider Legal, Compliance, Policy and Risk department. We are looking for someone who already has solid experience in the field. As well as a drive and attention to detail required to effectively conduct financial crime investigations. You will be supported by experienced team members and other cross\-functional areas to ensure that you have access to information and tools to carry out our financial crime fighting! **As our Financial Crime Investigator, you will:** * Be responsible for conducting transaction monitoring and sanctions screening checks for TrueLayer's customers * Monitor transactions for suspicious activity, based on prioritisation/risk. * Review sanctions alerts to determine whether they are true positives or not and document your rationale. * Complete investigations, collating information from a range of sources to make informed decisions. * Raise and submit accurate and specific Requests for Information (RFI’s) to the firm's merchants and other third parties as required. * Drafting accurate and timely Suspicious Activity Reports (SARs / STR’s) for review and subsequent submission to the relevant FIU. * Articulate findings in comprehensive reports, identifying financial crime risks and making concise recommendations. * Assist in scoping new monitoring scenarios that may be required and fine tune existing scenarios. * Identify financial crime typologies and trends and communicate these to the wider team. * Assist in investigating reports of fraud. * Assist with dealing with law enforcement enquiries and liaise with authorities to provide the information required. * Contribute to the ongoing improvement of systems and processes. * Be comfortable working to specific delivery targets, as well as setting KPIs and support in the accurate collation of MI for the MLRO / external reporting. **Requirements** **What we expect from you:** * Candidates should have a degree, minimum 1\-2 years relevant Financial Crime experience. * An understanding of different legal entities, business and customer types. * You should have a basic understanding of money laundering laws and typologies across a range of products and services/ different customer types. * Some knowledge of Financial Crime risks, AML controls and regulatory standards. * Comfortable working with other functions and challenging or escalating, where appropriate. * Excellent written and oral communication skills * Attention to detail, interested in learning and engaging with others. **We would be really excited if you have:** * Experience performing transaction monitoring reviews for eCommerce Merchants, and/or iGaming/Gambling businesses preferable. * Law or business degree 2:1 or above * Understanding of UK and EU AML/CTF legislation (international AML/CTF knowledge is advantageous) * Have a recognised Financial Crime qualification. * Experience in Finance, a Financial Technology company or a Technology company; * Experience in a hyper growth technology startup. **Benefits** **What you can expect from us: (please note for FTCs some benefits will be prorated in accordance with the contract period)** * Flexible hours and hybrid working. Split your time 50/50 between working from home and our welcoming offices in Dublin 🇮🇪 spending 2\-3 days per week in the office. * Need to collect the kids from childcare? title: Forward Deployed Engineer IV, Generative AI, Google Cloud
description: **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, a related field, or equivalent practical experience. * 8 years of experience in cloud computing or a technical customer\-facing role. * Experience taking production\-grade AI\-driven solutions from conception to launch and architecting AI systems on cloud platforms (e.g., Google Cloud Platform (GCP)). * Experience building pipelines for structured and unstructured data using both vector databases and RAG\-like architectures to power enterprise AI solutions. * Experience leading technical discovery sessions. **Preferred qualifications:** * Master’s or PhD in AI, Computer Science, or a related technical field. * Experience implementing multi\-agent systems using frameworks (e.g., LangGraph, CrewAI, ADK) and complex patterns (e.g., ReAct, self\-reflection, hierarchical delegation). * Knowledge of "LLM\-native" metrics (e.g., tokens/sec, cost\-per\-request) and techniques for optimizing state management and granular tracing. **About the job** As a GenAI Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE) at Google Cloud, you are an embedded builder who bridges the gap between frontier AI products and production\-grade reality within customers. You will function as an "innovator\-builder," moving beyond high\-level architecture to code, debug, and jointly ship bespoke agentic solutions directly within the customer’s environment. Your role is designed for high\-agency engineers with a founder’s mindset. You will address blockers to production including solving the integration complexities, data readiness issues, and state\-management issues that prevent AI from reaching enterprise\-grade maturity, by embedding with strategic accounts, you will serve a dual purpose: providing "white glove" deployment of AI systems and acting as a critical feedback loop, transforming real\-world field insights into Google Cloud’s future product roadmap.It's an exciting time to join Google Cloud’s Go\-To\-Market team, leading the AI revolution for businesses worldwide. You’ll leverage Google's brand credibility—a legacy built on inventing foundational technologies and proven at scale. We’ll provide you with the world's most advanced AI portfolio, including frontier Gemini models, and the complete Vertex AI platform, helping you to solve business problems. We’re a collaborative culture providing direct access to DeepMind's engineering and research minds, empowering you to solve customer challenges. Join us to be the catalyst for our mission, drive customer success, and define the new cloud era—the market is yours. **Responsibilities** * Serve as a developer for AI applications, transitioning from rapid prototypes to production\-grade agentic workflows (e.g., multi\-agent systems, Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers) that drive measurable Return on Investment (ROI). * Architect and code the "connective tissue" between Google’s AI products and customer's live infrastructure, including APIs, legacy data silos, and security perimeters as part of an expert team. * Build high\-performance evaluation pipelines and observability frameworks to ensure agentic systems meet rigorous requirements for accuracy, safety, and latency. * Identify repeatable field patterns and friction points in Google’s AI stack, converting them into reusable modules or formal product feature requests for the Engineering teams. * Co\-build with customer engineering teams to instill Google\-grade development best practices, ensuring long\-term project success and high end\-user adoption. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

title: Fund Accountant
description: At U.S. Bank, we’re on a journey to do our best. Helping the customers and businesses we serve to make better and smarter financial decisions and enabling the communities we support to grow and succeed. We believe it takes all of us to bring our shared ambition to life, and each person is unique in their potential. A career with U.S. Bank gives you a wide, ever\-growing range of opportunities to discover what makes you thrive at every stage of your career. Try new things, learn new skills and discover what you excel at—all from Day One. We actively uphold transparent and fair hiring practices that support individual opportunity, inclusive culture, and career mobility across all levels of our organisation. We offer meaningful opportunities for growth, a culture of inclusion, and a strong commitment to transparency and integrity in everything we do. **Job Description** ------------------- Alternative Investments Associates and Accountants work as part of a team responsible for providing accounting, administration and investor servicing for hedge funds, private equity funds and closed\-end registered products. Individuals calculate net asset value(s) and disseminate financial and performance information to the investment advisor and their investors. Acting as primary fund accountant on multiple relationships, ensures all fund accounting functions are in completed in accordance with Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP). Records trade information, income/expense activity and applies valuation of securities in the portfolio accounting system. Enters and reconciles all investor contributions and withdrawals in the partnership accounting system. Allocates fund level profits to individual investors for preparation and distribution of investor statements. Reviews and coordinates fund expense analysis and expense processing. Prepares fund financial statements, audit and tax schedules. Basic Qualifications * High school diploma or equivalent Preferred Skills/Experience * Basic knowledge of Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) * Ability to identify and resolve/escalate complex problems with minimal guidance * Ability to manage multiple tasks/projects and deadlines simultaneously * Effective interpersonal, verbal and written communication skills * Proficient computer navigation skills using a variety of software packages including Microsoft Office applications **Accessibility** We are committed to providing an inclusive and accessible recruitment experience. If you need adjustments at any stage of the application or hiring process, please contact your recruiter for guidance and support. **Total Rewards** U.S. Bank is committed to fair, equitable, and transparent compensation practices in line with local regulatory and legal requirements. Our total rewards approach is designed to attract, retain, and support top talent while ensuring equal pay for work of equal value. We offer a market\-competitive compensation package that includes: * **Clearly defined salary ranges** aligned with industry benchmarks and internal equity standards. * **Performance\-based incentives** for eligible employees (as defined by relevant plan rules), awarded through transparent, objective criteria that recognize both individual and company performance. * **Inclusive equitable benefits** that are accessible to all employees and focused around our 3 main pillars of financial wellbeing, health \& wellness). * **Continuous development opportunities** including training, education support, and career progression pathways based on inclusive and transparent criteria. * **Employee recognition programs** that celebrate achievements and milestones for all. We regularly review our compensation and benefits to ensure they remain competitive, inclusive, and responsive to employee needs and market trends. Further details of the compensation package will be provided upon application. We encourage candidates to explore the full value of our offer, including monetary and non\-monetary benefits, at Employee benefits and development \| U.S. Bank \| Elavon. **Closing Date** Posting may be closed earlier due to high volume of applicants. We aim to provide timely updates throughout the process and encourage early applications to ensure consideration.

title: General Operative
description: **Seasonal General Operatives (Fixed\-Term Contracts 2026\-2027\)** **Lir Chocolates, Navan, County Meath, Ireland** Join the team at Lir Chocolates for our 2026\-2027 seasonal production period! We’re recruiting General Operatives on fixed\-term contracts to support our busy chocolate manufacturing site. As a General Operative, you’ll work in either production or packing, contributing to the creation of our high‑quality chocolate products. You'll operate on a factory line, follow food safety standards, maintain excellent hygiene and work closely with your team to meet daily targets. **Shift Patterns Available** * Days \- Monday to Friday 0600\-1430 * Days \- Monday to Thursday 0600\-1630 * Nights \- Monday to Friday 1430\-0100 * Nights \- Monday to Thursday 1630 \-0300 * Weekend Nights \- Friday to Sunday 1800\-0630 * Weekend Days \- Friday to Sunday 0600\-1830 **Pay rate** €14\.15 per hour plus 4% shift premium for weekend days and 8% shift premium for weekend nights. * Operating production line machinery safely and efficiently (full training provided). * Packing, weighing, and labelling products to company standards. * Monitoring product quality and reporting any defects or issues promptly. * Maintaining a clean, hygienic, and organised work area. * Following all food safety and hygiene regulations. * Carrying out routine cleaning of equipment and production areas. * Assisting with loading, unloading, and moving stock when required. * Accurately completing production and quality records. * Working collaboratively with your team to achieve daily output targets. * Following instructions from team leaders and supervisors. * Being flexible and willing to support different areas of production as business needs change. * Adhering to all health and safety guidelines at all times. * Ability to work on your own initiative while being a strong team player. * A positive, proactive attitude and reliable approach to work. * Good communication skills \- verbal, written, and reading. * Punctual, hardworking, and committed. * 25 days annual leave \+ bank holiday's (pro\-rata based on shift pattern) * 1\.5% company\-matched employee pension plan * Death in service benefit * Employee Assistance Programme * Training and development opportunities Lir Chocolates operates across two modern buildings in the Johnstown IDA Industrial Park, Navan (C15 CY05\). The site has a proud history of producing award‑winning chocolates and holds a long‑standing partnership with Diageo, manufacturing the well‑known Baileys and Guinness chocolate ranges sold worldwide. With three main production lines and around 150 employees, our workforce can double in size during peak seasonal periods to meet demand. We use a variety of specialist techniques, including panning, one‑shot production, spinning, hand‑decorating, spraying, and transfer technologies. The factory has benefited from significant investment in recent years to support growing demand, and many of our packaging formats are unique and hand‑assembled, creating hands‑on, varied work across both sites. Please apply directly through the job advert. You will be directed to complete a full application on the Lir Chocolates website, where you must answer all required questions and upload your CV. Current Fixed Term contracts are expected to run until around December 2026, but there will be possibility of extending them past this time depending on business requirement. If you are selected, you will receive an email inviting you to book an interview time slot with a member of our team. Interviews take place on site, where you’ll meet us and answer a short series of questions about your experience and suitability. If successful, you will then be invited to attend one of our induction sessions. All details will be sent to you by email, including what to bring, what documents you need and any tasks to complete in advance. After attending induction, you will be allocated a start date and advised of your first shift. Lir Chocolates is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. If you require any adjustments or support during the application or interview process, please let our Talent Acquisition team know at the earliest stage.

title: General Operative (Morning, Evening & Night shifts)
description: **Your Recipe For a Rewarding Career Starts Here!** **Why Dawn Farms?** We can offer an excellent opportunity to be part of a world\-class team offering permanent, full\-time positions with set hours and plenty of opportunity for further training, development and career opportunities should you wish to progress further. Our culture is one of inclusiveness and diversity with a constant drive and focus for continuous improvement throughout our business. Our production plants are located in Naas, Co Kildare with easy access via public transport. **About Us** Dawn Farms is the largest dedicated supplier of **cooked meat and plant\-based ingredients and fermented products** outside of the US supplying world leading brands across more than 44 markets from our two state of the art plant facilities based in Naas. We also have manufacturing facilities in the UK and Germany. Dawn Farms is one of the largest employers in Naas, Co Kildare with a strong culture of diversity and inclusion. **Job Purpose** Dawn Farms is currently seeking for **General Operatives** to join our **Production Teams.** You will play an integral part of the team in ensuring the highest level of hygiene, food safety and health and safety standards are met. **Positions:** Full\-Time **Location:** Naas, Co. Kildare. **Salary:** €14\.15 \- €18\.39 per hour (overtime opportunities available) **Role Responsibilities \& Duties** * Clean tools, equipment and work surfaces. * Pack and prepare meat. * Dispatch and palletize. * Production line work. * Comply with SOPs. * Adhere to company health and safety policies/procedures. * Adhere to hygiene and food safety measures. **Skills \& Experience** * Be a team player. * Be comfortable working with meat \& in a chiller environment. * Highly motivated and enthusiastic. * Ability to take instruction but also ability to work on own initiative. * Be health and safety conscious. * Flexible to the needs of the business. **What we can offer you?** * Annual leave 171\.60 hours. * Overtime opportunity \& Shift premiums where applicable. * Pension plan. * Health insurance contribtuion plan. * Subsidised canteen. * Employee assistance programme (EAP) \- this programme offers expert wellbeing support 24 hours a day 365 days a year access to counselling, financial information, consumer information, life coaching, health information, autism support, parent coaching, International employee support, all free for you and your family. * Free English classes. * Wellness programme. * Maternity/Paternity leave benefits. * Training \& Development, Career progression. * Christmas Hamper. * Christmas Savings Scheme. * Bike to work scheme.

title: General Operative / GO
description: General OperativePrefabrication Facility – Kells, Co. Meath**Location:** Kells, Co. Meath **Company:** Designer Group **Job type:** Full\-time, permanent **Department:** Off\-Site Manufacturing / Prefabrication About Designer Group Designer Group is a leading international mechanical, electrical and instrumentation engineering contractor. We deliver major projects across Ireland, the UK and Europe, supporting sectors including data centres, life sciences, industrial, commercial, energy and infrastructure. As we continue to grow, we are developing our new prefabrication facility in **Kells, Co. Meath**. This facility will play a key role in how we deliver projects more efficiently, safely and sustainably by manufacturing and assembling high\-quality M\&E components off site before they are delivered to projects. We are now looking for **General Operatives** to join the team and support the day\-to\-day production, assembly and movement of materials within the facility. The Role This is a hands\-on role suited to someone who is reliable, hardworking and comfortable working in a busy production or construction\-related environment. You will support the fabrication, assembly, preparation and movement of materials and components within the facility. You do not need to be a qualified tradesperson, but experience in construction, manufacturing, warehousing, logistics, mechanical, electrical or general labouring environments would be beneficial. Full training will be provided where required. Key Responsibilities As a General Operative, you will: * Support the assembly, preparation and movement of prefabricated M\&E components. * Assist trades, supervisors and production teams with daily tasks. * Load, unload, sort and move materials safely around the facility. * Prepare work areas, tools, parts and materials for production activities. * Carry out basic assembly, fitting, packing, labelling and general production duties. * Help maintain a clean, safe and organised working environment. * Follow health and safety procedures at all times. * Report any defects, shortages, damage or safety concerns to the supervisor. * Support stock control, housekeeping and material handling activities. * Work as part of a team to meet production targets and project deadlines. * Operate basic tools, equipment or machinery where trained and authorised to do so. * Carry out any other reasonable duties required to support the successful running of the facility. **Requirements** We are looking for people who are dependable, practical and willing to learn. You should have: * Previous experience in a general operative, labouring, warehouse, production, manufacturing or construction environment. * A strong work ethic and positive attitude. * Good attention to detail. * Ability to follow instructions and work safely. * Good timekeeping and reliability. * Ability to work well as part of a team. * Willingness to learn new skills. * Manual handling awareness or certification would be an advantage. * Forklift, MEWP or safe pass certification would be beneficial but is not essential. * Experience working around mechanical, electrical, pipework, steelwork, cable containment or modular assembly would be an advantage. Skills and Attributes* Reliable and punctual. * Safety\-conscious. * Practical and hands\-on. * Team player. * Flexible and willing to support different tasks. * Good communicator. * Organised and tidy in approach to work. * Comfortable working in a busy facility environment. **Benefits** This is an excellent opportunity to join Designer Group at an exciting stage as we develop our new prefabrication facility in Kells. You will be part of a growing team supporting major engineering projects across Ireland, the UK and Europe. At Designer Group, we offer: * Long\-term career opportunities. * A stable role with a growing international engineering contractor. * Training and development. * Exposure to modern off\-site manufacturing and prefabrication methods. * A strong team environment. * The opportunity to be part of a new facility from an early stage. * Work that directly supports major construction and engineering projects. Apply Now If you are reliable, hardworking and interested in joining a growing prefabrication team in Kells, we would like to hear from you. **Apply today to join Designer Group as a General Operative.**

process** for Manufacturing, ensuring that initiatives are clearly defined, prioritized, and aligned to business value, operational impact, and regulatory requirements. * Works in close partnership operations organizations at a global level, with a proven track record of shaping and delivering technology\-enabled business outcomes * Strong understanding of manufacturing systems and architecture, technology investment cases, with strong financial acumen and the ability to prioritise initiatives based on business value, return on investment, and strategic impact. * Experience establishing and leading or contributing to large\-scale, multi\-site or global technology programmes within manufacturing environments, ensuring consistency, scalability, and successful adoption across diverse title: Graduate Mechanical Design Engineer
description: **Role Purpose** The role of a Graduate Mechanical Design Engineer is to execute engineering and design activities of the project. To conduct design reviews, project engineering, commissioning support ensuring consistent application of agreed procedures and utilization of standard forms. Graduate Mechanical Design Engineer’s Roles \& Responsibilities **General** * Assist and conduct the mechanical engineering and design activities on the project. * Support mechanical package owners. * Ensure the design management process is being followed on the project by either taking on the role directly. **EHS** * Read and understand EHS Plan. * Read, understand and promote the mechanical safe systems of work (MSSW) **Procurement** * Assist in the production of pre\-qualification packs (PQP) for design services and RFQ’s for equipment. Forward to project quantity surveyor for Subcontractor Package or Procurement Manager for Purchases. **RFQ Shall Include** * Scope of Works * Drawings and Specifications * Compliance, Deviation and Exclusion Matrices * Schedule \- deliveries, installation and commissioning requirements PQQ shall be in line with MP17 \- Selection and appointment of design consultants. * Mark\-up equipment and design services quotes to advise the project manager of the correct scope that aligns with the technical submittal and design brief. * Develop and update procurement \& design schedule/tracker with project planner. Continuously update the tracker until all technical submittals achieve status A, design deliverables accepted and materials/equipment have been delivered. **Quality** * Be engaged and utilize the documentation workflow for the project. Documentation generation, peer review, submission, re\-submission, distribution etc. * Review, comment and understand Quality Plan. * Assist in the development the project scope specific ITP considering project specification, client and local requirements. * Assist in the development of system tracker including equipment detail, interfacing details (electrical \& control), commissioning checklists and walkdown requirements * Review and understand any local authority requirements. * Develop lessons learned log of the project and continually update. * Indicate what the learning was * What the outcome or mitigations were * Indicate whether it was an internal finding or should be reported externally * Indicate if it was a cost, time or both impacts. * Assist in the O\&Ms production in line with the specification and agreed program. **Planning** * Understand and provide input and updates to the project schedule. * Input ROJ dates of CFCI equipment onto the equipment tracker. * Request and monitor the OFCI delivery dates. **Engineering \& Design** * Complete familiarisation and technical reviews on projects and record notes. Pose questions to the estimators and submit relevant RFI’s for clarifications on design/ scope. * Assist in the development of systems tracker along with equipment tag references, details and interfaces. * Assist in the development and tracking of design responsibility matrix/ contractor design portion (CDP). * Assist with the development and input into Design Deviation and Exclusions Log to ensure the team are aware of the agreed deviations and exclusions from design. * Validate the design using the request for information (RFI), design review process and performing the appropriate high level technical calculations. * Assist in the development of schematic’s when gaps exist in the design. * Review and understand The BIM execution plan, the Commissioning Plan and the Tracking Plan for the project. * Review the federated BIM model. * Review the coordinated drawings for construction to ensure it is in compliance with the P\&ID’s and schematics and that there is sufficient access to plant and equipment for commissioning, maintenance and replacement. * Development and track Technical Submittals until Status A. * Assist in the management of change from a technical perspective, updating change logs and schedules as required. * Tag Devices and instrumentation, spot each device on site in consultation with controls vendor and designer. * Attend biweekly technical meetings with members of the design team, subcontractors and internal project engineering team. * Assist in the verification of pipe and pump, duct and fan sizing and ensure design selections are suitable. * Assist in the production of the O\&M manuals in compliance with the specification. * Execute the project design and engineering process to ensure compliance. * Assist in technical review of tenders when required. * Attend and contribute to engineering and design kick\-off meeting at the outset of the project and record the minutes when required. * Attend and contribute to weekly
