title: Graduate Sales Executive (Benelux Market)
description: **About Revolut** People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75\+ million customers get more from their money every day. As we continue our lightning\-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000\+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution. **About The Role** Our Sales team powers customer growth around the world. From prospecting to acquisition and activation, they own their market segments, solve complex problems, and help more customers discover the value of Revolut with a sharp, solution\-oriented approach. We're looking for a Sales Executive to join our Graduate Sales Programme, which offers an unparalleled opportunity for ambitious graduates, regardless of prior experience, to join a high\-performing, international team. You'll be at the forefront of delivering cutting\-edge financial solutions to customers while building key relationships. You’ll kick things off with a focused bootcamp, complete with workshops, lectures, and hands\-on training to sharpen your commercial and communication skills. Once you're up to speed, you'll dive straight into the on\-the\-job experience by working with real clients. Watch this video from our team members about what goes into being an Account Executive at Revolut. Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch. **What You'll Be Doing** * Outreaching prospects across multiple touch points and channels, with an emphasis on cold\-calling * Developing and executing a robust lead\-sourcing strategy by identifying key verticals, diversifying your prospects base, and maintaining consistent activity levels * Building and managing your own pipeline, from prospecting to closing and account management, maximising adoption and upsell/cross\-sell opportunities * Liaising customer relationships with internal stakeholders to ensure a smooth experience, from getting started to daily usage * Being autonomous to effectively balance your time (prospect vs. close vs. upsell), activate internal resources, and engage your team * Using solution\-based selling to understand customer needs and communicate the value proposition * Becoming a product expert on Revolut Business, its market positioning, and the solutions it offers, backed by relevant, compelling case studies **What You'll Need** * Fluency in Dutch and English * To have graduated from university in 2024 or 2025 * Excellent communication skills and articulation over the phone * Impeccable literacy skills (email, LinkedIn outreach) * A drive to launch a career in a high\-performing sales culture * An interest in the financial industry **Nice to have** * Previous internship or experience in a high\-performance, fast\-paced environment, with exposure to strategic thinking, analytical tasks, or client\-facing work Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D\&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard\-working team. **Important notice for candidates:** Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles. * Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third\-party services or platforms for our recruitment. * Always double\-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain. **We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process.** If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately. By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

title: HR & Payroll Generalist
description: Be a part of our mission! As a world leader in creating comfortable, sustainable, and efficient climate solutions for buildings, homes and transportation, it's our responsibility to put the planet first. For us at Trane Technologies, and through our businesses including Trane® and Thermo King, sustainability is not just how we do business—it is our business. Do you dare to look at the world's challenges and see impactful possibilities? Do you want to contribute to making a better future? If the answer is yes, we invite you to consider joining us in boldly challenging what's possible for a sustainable world. Learn about our benefits designed for you to Thrive at work and at home. We boldly go. **Where Is The Work** Monday to Thursday, work onsite with your colleagues. Fridays, choose your work location, balancing what your work requires. **HR \& Payroll Generalist** **Location:** Swords, Dublin **Reports to:** HR Leader Ireland **Company Overview** Trane Technologies is a global climate innovator bringing efficient and sustainable climate solutions to buildings, homes, and transportation. As a leader in climate technology, we create innovative solutions that matter for people and our planet, for generations to come. **Position Summary** We are seeking an HR Generalist to provide day‑to‑day HR partnership and operational support for our Corporate functions based in Swords, Dublin. Supporting a population of approximately 90 employees in a stand\-alone HR role, this position plays an important part in ensuring consistent, high‑quality HR service delivery across the full employee lifecycle. A key responsibility of this role is the coordination and oversight of three\-monthly payroll cycles, delivered by our outsourced payroll provider, ADP. The ideal candidate will bring a strong commitment to accuracy and process excellence, alongside an ability to work collaboratively in a matrix environment with multiple stakeholders. You will also serve as a member of the Swords site leadership team, contributing to and influencing the local people agenda in partnership with cross‑functional leaders. **Key Responsibilities** **Payroll Operations** You will be the operational lead for payroll delivery for the salaried employees in Ireland (approx. 320 employees). * Coordinate end‑to‑end payroll activities across three monthly cycles in partnership with ADP. * Prepare, validate, and reconcile payroll inputs including hiring changes, terminations, compensation updates, and leave information. * Review payroll reports for accuracy and ensure timely approvals. * Manage the day‑to‑day relationship with ADP, ensuring reliable service delivery and effective issue resolution. * Partner with Finance and HR colleagues to ensure accurate reporting, compliance, and audit readiness. **Support Employees and Managers** You will help employees and leaders navigate people processes and policies with confidence and clarity. * Serve as a primary point of contact for HR policy, process, and systems queries. * Provide guidance on performance, attendance, and employee relations matters, escalating appropriately. * Foster trusted, collaborative relationships that strengthen the employee experience. **Enable Talent Processes and Onboarding** You will help ensure a positive and consistent experience for candidates and new employees. * Support full‑cycle recruitment for employees onsite. * Coordinate interview logistics, offer activities, and onboarding processes to create a seamless transition for new hires. * Contribute to key people processes such as onboarding, performance cycles, and engagement activities. **Deliver HR Operations And Benefits Administration** You will help maintain the integrity and efficiency of our HR operations. * Maintain employee data accuracy in Workday, UKG and other HR systems. * Support administration of benefits programs, including enrolments and employee inquiries. * Ensure adherence to Irish employment legislation and internal HR policies. * Contribute to projects and process improvements that enhance HR services. **What You Bring** * Bachelor’s degree in human resources, business administration, or a related field. * Experience in an HR Generalist role, ideally within a multinational or matrixed environment. * Strong understanding of Irish employment legislation. * Proven experience coordinating payroll with outsourced providers (ADP experience and IPASS certification is an advantage). * Ability to influence leaders and guide decision making through clear, practical HR insights. * Comfortable working in a standalone HR capacity, demonstrating sound judgment, independence, and confidence in navigating day to day HR matters. * High attention to detail, strong organisational skills, and confident working with data and systems. * Effective communication and relationship\-building skills. **What We Offer** At Trane Technologies, we are committed to supporting your growth, wellbeing, and success. You will join a purpose‑driven company with a strong focus on inclusion, innovation, and sustainability, and a team that values collaboration, curiosity, and continuous improvement. **You Can Expect** * Competitive compensation and comprehensive benefits. * Opportunities for learning, development, and career growth within a global organization. * A supportive and inclusive work environment where your contributions make a meaningful impact. * The chance to help shape the employee experience and people agenda for the Swords site. We offer competitive compensation and comprehensive benefits and programs. We are an equal opportunity employer; all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, pregnancy, age, marital status, disability, status as a protected veteran, or any legally protected status.

title: IT & Continuous Improvement Assistant
description: **Reports to:** Continuous Improvement Lead **Job Purpose:** Support the day\-to\-day IT needs of the factory while assisting with continuous improvement, digitalisation, training and process standardisation projects **Responsibilities associated with this role include, but are not limited to:** **Basic IT Tasks:** * Provide first\-line support for IT\-related issues within the factory environment * Troubleshoot hardware, software, printer, scanner, mobile device and basis network issues * Escalate complex issues to Group IT or external providers where required * Support users with Microsoft 365, shared drives, factory systems and standard business applications * Assist with routine IT checks, updates and maintenance activities in line with company procedures and Group IT guidance * Support the development and testing of simple automation, reporting and data exchange improvements between existing factory systems **Continuous Improvement \& Digitalisation Support:** * Support continuous improvement projects across production, operations, quality, engineering and training * Assist with process mapping, data collection, problem solving and action tracking * Help identify opportunities to reduce manual administration, improve information flow and standardise ways of working * Support the development of simple digital tools, forms, trackers, reports and dashboards * Assist with the rollout of factory improvement projects, ensuring users are trained and supported **Operational Training:** * Assist in operational training sessions for new starters, providing guidance and support as needed to facilitate smooth onboarding processes **IT Asset Management:** * Maintain an accurate inventory of all IT assets, including hardware, software licenses, and peripherals * Track the procurement, deployment, and retirement of IT assets according to established procedures and budgetary constraints * Conduct periodic audits to verify the physical location and condition of IT assets, reconciling discrepancies as necessary * Support the development and testing of simple automation, reporting and data exchange improvements between existing factory systems * Professional development is encouraged to support career growth and progression **Qualifications/ Skills/ Experience:** **Essential:** * Demonstrated proficiency in basic IT tasks including hardware and software troubleshooting, and system maintenance. Ideally 1 year experience in IT environment * Strong organisational skills with the ability to prioritise tasks, manage time effectively, and handle multiple responsibilities simultaneously * Show initiative and self\-motivation * Demonstrate a strong aptitude for problem\-solving * Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to effectively convey technical information and collaborate with cross\-functional teams * Proven ability to identify, analyse, and resolve IT\-related issues in a timely and efficient manner, minimising disruption to operations **Desirable:** * Experience in a manufacturing, food production or regulated environment * Familiarity with continuous improvement tools such as 5S, Lean, root cause analysis, standard work or process mapping * Experience with SharePoint, Power Apps, Power BI, Excel reporting or similar digital tools * Experience creating training materials, SOP’s videos or presentations * Knowledge of IT asset management, inventory control or software licence tracking * Understanding of basic IT security practices * Experience supporting system rollouts, user training or change management * Recognition of Prior Learning (RPL) may be considered, based on relevant experience, training, or voluntary work Pay: €35,500\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Employee assistance program * On\-site parking Application question(s): * Do you have experience providing first\-line IT support to end users? * What Microsoft applications and systems have you used regularly? * Have you been involved in any process improvement or continuous improvement projects? * Are you comfortable providing hands\-on support on a factory floor, including troubleshooting PCs, printers, scanners, tablets, and user issues? Work Location: In person

title: IT & Digital Operations Specialist
description: Vacancy Number: 28513 Category: IT \- Digital Delivery Job Type: Full\-time **Position Description** The eMobility Systems Operations Specialist, reporting to the IT \& Digital Operations Lead, is responsible for ensuring the effective operation of the integrated landscape of IT \& Digital services that are essential to the ESB ecars business and its customers on a 24/7 basis. The technology landscape includes a charge point management and revenue management system (SaaS), integrations with third\-party systems (e.g., payment gateways, roaming platforms, CRM and business partners), and customer touchpoints such as digital tools, mobile applications and RFID cards. The role requires broad, hands\-on experience across digital/web/mobile applications, databases, IoT and computer networking, combined with strong service management, troubleshooting and organisational skills and a customer\-centric mindset. ESB ecars installs, operates and manages publicly available EV charge point infrastructure across the Republic of Ireland, Northern Ireland and Great Britain. As the business scales rapidly through the roll\-out of fast and high\-power charging, this role supports that growth by keeping critical customer\-facing systems and integrations operating effectively and enabling continuous improvement of the digital services that underpin the charging experience. In support of ESB ecars’ growth ambitions and the delivery of a best\-in\-class customer experience, you will help ensure the availability, reliability and effective operation of the end\-to\-end eMobility IT \& Digital services (including charge point management, revenue management, payments, roaming and customer\-facing digital channels) and support the safe introduction of new features and integrations. Working closely with the IT \& Digital Operations Lead, the Operations team, service providers and business partners, you will provide 3rd level support, coordinate service management activities, and contribute to improved operational resilience, cyber security controls and data management across the ESB ecars technology ecosystem. **Key Responsibilities** * Business applications functional support and operation, manage and support ecars business applications including charge point management, revenue management, payment gateways, interfaces and customer\-facing digital applications. * Maintain network and systems availability by working with Operations, service providers and roaming partners to ensure high availability and utilisation of the charging networks across ROI, NI and GB. * Provide 3rd level customer operations troubleshooting in collaboration with the Operations team and partners (e.g., billing, charge point communications, payments and international roaming). * Coordinate service management activities day\-to\-day: monitor provider performance and manage incident/issue tracking, change scheduling, and outage planning/management. * Facilitate connection and on\-boarding of new charge point models, e\-mobility roaming platforms and e\-Mobility Service Providers with field staff, service providers and partners. * Draw up and execute test plans to verify and validate enhancements/new feature sets and manage their transition into production with minimal business disruption. * Implement operational and technical controls (e.g., patches, security configurations) to address cyber security risks and support NIS Directive compliance, working closely with vendors for assurance. **Experience and Qualifications** **Essential** * Third level Degree in Computer Science, Information Systems or Engineering, or equivalent work experience. * 5\+ years’ relevant experience. * Broad experience implementing and supporting digital systems using cloud platforms (e.g., Azure and Google Cloud), web services and APIs, and functional areas such as customer billing, payment processing and CRM. * Strong service management experience (incident, problem and vendor management) with good understanding of communications/networking and familiarity with information security and cyber security standards and regulations (including NISD). **Desirable** * Relevant certifications/experience such as ITIL 4 Foundation/Managing Professional, CISSP (or related), mobile technologies, and knowledge of e\-mobility/energy systems and standards (e.g., OCPP and OCPI). **Location** Swift Square, Santry, Dublin. *Smart Working at ESB is designed to make ‘in\-person’ time together as purposeful as possible while also enabling the effective use of digital tools and hybrid arrangements to preserve our commitment to flexibility. The successful candidate may work flexibly within the jurisdiction where they have been employed, with attendance at the workplace for in\-person collaboration at least* ***two*** *days per week. All Smart Working arrangements are subject to regular review based on the operational requirements of the role, the team, and the business.* **Reporting To** IT \& Digital Operations Lead, ESB ecars, Customer Solutions **Why Work with Us?** * Opportunity to lead Ireland’s transition to net zero carbon future * Career development through mentoring and training * Corporate Social Responsibility Opportunities * Sports and Social Clubs * Networking opportunities * Credit Union * Generous Pension * Access to staff well\-being programmes * Generous parental leave entitlements * Strong values\-based and inclusive culture * Strong commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion * Great team environment working to our Core Values: Courageous, Caring, Driven and Trusted **Salary** €59,900 \- €70,500 per annum (depending on experience) **Closing Date** 18th June 2026 **Interview** Candidates who are shortlisted for interview will complete a Behavioural Interview based on each of the following Behaviours, which underpin how we work at ESB and are critical to delivering our strategy: * Accountable and Ethical * Collaborative * Growing and Adaptive * Proactive * Respectful and Supportive More information available on Our People Promise **Notes** Your application will be held in reserve for 6 months should you be suitable for the role. The successful candidate will be required to evidence their right to work in the Republic of Ireland prior to commencement of employment. **Diversity, Equity and Inclusion Statement** ESB is committed to being an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from all sections of society and ensure that no one is discriminated against on the grounds of race, religion or belief, ethnicity or nationality, disability, age, citizenship, marital status, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation or gender identity, or any other basis as protected by law. Applicants who anticipate requiring assistance or reasonable accommodations for any part of the application or interview process may contact, in confidence, recruitment@esb.ie. **About ESB** Join us in our mission to achieve a net\-zero electricity system by 2040\. ESB is Ireland’s leading energy utility, with activities spanning electricity generation, transmission and distribution, energy supply, energy services and international consultancy. Operating in Ireland, Northern Ireland and Great Britain, we invest over €1 billion each year to deliver a new energy future based on reliable, affordable zero\-carbon electricity.

title: IT Service Management Analyst
description: **BASIC PURPOSE:** The Purpose of the IT Service Management Analyst is to Provide full range of reporting on IT services supplied to the business. Ensure that major service issues are recorded, tracked and (in conjunction with the dept. manager) addressed effectively across the local and regional organisation. **PRINCIPAL ACCOUNTABILITIES:** The principal accountabilities of the role will be: * Provide effective and timely reporting related to service level attainment * Major issues * Outages and escalations * Initiate escalations if/when required to vendors (ITS, HP. BT etc.) * Help to determine the root cause of issues, liaising with required support teams across the IT organisation * Use service reporting to identify trends and highlight positive movements or the need for action to improve service performance * Be customer service focused at all times in delivering IT services to the business **Skills / Qualifications:** * Leaving Cert Level * Good Microsoft Office skills * Strong communication (written and oral) * Good organisational skills and ability to work on own initiative and within a team critical * ITIL Foundation beneficial * Ability to upskill

title: IT Support Engineer L1/L2 - MSP
description: **Level 1 / Level 2 IT Support Engineer – MSP** **South County Dublin \| 5 days office\-based \| MSP experience required** A small, well\-established MSP in **South County Dublin** is hiring a **Level 1 / Level 2 IT Support Engineer** to join its service desk team. This role is for someone with around **2–3 years’ IT support experience gained in an MSP environment** . MSP experience is required, as you will be supporting multiple clients, managing ticket flow, switching between different environments, and troubleshooting at pace. This is a **5 days per week office\-based role** . This is non\-negotiable. You must already be **living in South County Dublin** , and you must be **fully legal to work in Ireland now** . There is **no visa sponsorship and no relocation support** for this role. Most of the work will be service desk based, supporting clients remotely, but there may be occasional site visits when needed. This is not a field engineer role, but you will need to be presentable, client\-focused, and comfortable visiting client sites from time to time. **What you’ll be doing** * Managing and resolving support tickets across multiple clients * Troubleshooting Windows desktop, user, application and connectivity issues * Supporting Microsoft 365, Entra ID / Active Directory and user account issues * Helping users with MFA, Authenticator apps and access\-related problems * Basic network troubleshooting across TCP/IP, DNS and related issues * Supporting security tools and processes such as EDR, MDR and general cyber security hygiene * Communicating clearly with clients by phone, email and ticket updates * Escalating issues properly when needed * Carrying out occasional client site visits when required **What you’ll need** * Around **2–3 years’ IT support experience** * Previous **MSP experience** — this is required * Strong Level 1 support ability, with some Level 2 troubleshooting experience * Good Windows troubleshooting experience * Good understanding of TCP/IP, DNS and basic networking * Experience with Active Directory and/or Entra ID * Exposure to Microsoft 365 * Awareness of MFA, Authenticator apps, EDR/MDR and general cyber security basics * Strong written and verbal communication skills * A positive, can\-do attitude and willingness to keep learning * Good troubleshooting instincts and the ability to move quickly between issues * You must be living in **South County Dublin** * Access to your own car or motorbike would be very useful, but it is not essential This role would suit someone who enjoys the variety of MSP work, wants exposure to different clients and technologies, and is happy working in a small, flexible team. Salary for this Level 1 Technical Support Engineer role ranges from €35,000 \- €39,000 depending on your experience. Apply with your CV if you experience as a Technical Support Engineer and your location fits the requirements.

title: IT/OT Telecoms Network Architect
description: We are the people behind Ireland’s electricity grid. We operate the power system with world\-leading levels of variable asynchronous renewable generation, ensuring that everyone has power when they need it. But that’s not all — we are now transforming the power system for future generations. Our purpose is clear: to enable society to reduce carbon emissions and achieve climate change and net\-zero ambitions. This transformation requires innovation, resilience, and leadership in designing and deploying next\-generation OT and IT architectures that underpin critical infrastructure. **The Team** EirGrid Group has a unique role to play in leading this radical transformation of our power system in support of the transition to renewable energy. This will require pioneering engineering effort together with enhanced IT capability to respond successfully to the scale of disruption. This offers a unique opportunity to further your career in Telecom and Network Architecture while playing a significant role in delivering EirGrid Group’s Infrastructure transformation. We’re a Networking intensive business, and we operate a state\-of\-the\-art, specialised business\-critical systems, 24/7\. **The Opportunity** We are seeking an experienced **Network Architect** to join our growing team and play a key role in shaping the future of Ireland’s energy infrastructure. As we transition to a low\-carbon energy future, this is a unique opportunity to operate at a strategic level, influencing network architecture for both current operations and future requirements, including new interconnector projects and offshore renewable deployments. The Network Architect will be pivotal in designing, implementing, and governing the telecommunications and network architecture that underpins EirGrid’s critical operations. With ambitious plans to deploy offshore renewable energy generators over the next five years, there is an urgent need to enhance Network capabilities to support telemetry, SCADA, and Corporate assets and associated services for these installations. **Key Responsibilities*** Define and maintain the OT/IT network architecture roadmap, ensuring alignment with organisational strategy and regulatory requirements. * Lead the design and deployment of secure, high\-performance network solutions for critical infrastructure, including offshore renewable projects and interconnectors. * Oversee integration of OT and IT systems, ensuring interoperability, resilience, and compliance with cybersecurity standards. * Collaborate with offshore teams and external partners to deliver consistent architecture standards and accelerate project timelines. * Provide technical leadership and governance across network design, implementation, and operational support. * Develop and enforce architecture principles, policies, and best practices, including automation and standardisation. * Support business continuity and disaster recovery planning for network\-related services. * Act as a trusted advisor to senior stakeholders, influencing decisions on technology investments and solution approaches. **About You*** A recognised qualification in a relevant technical discipline supported by 10 years’ experience in senior network engineering or architecture roles within critical infrastructure environments. * Expert Knowledge of CISCO product suite Nexus, Catalyst, especially switching and routing * Proven expertise in OT networking, routing, switching, and WAN technologies (including MPLS) Port channels, VPC’s, Physical infrastructure. * Strong knowledge of network security, vulnerability management, and remediation. * Experience in architecting and delivering complex network solutions, including cloud and hybrid environments. * Demonstrated ability to develop operational procedures and run books, with a focus on automation. * Excellent technical and analytical skills, with the ability to operate effectively in complex environments. * Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to influence and collaborate across teams. Desirable * Professional certifications such as CCNP, CCIE, Azure Network Engineer Associate, or equivalent. * Experience with network automation tools and scripting (e.g., Python, Ansible, Netbox, OpsManage\+). **Closing date for applications** 08/06/2026 Please note we receive a high volume of applications, and as every application is carefully reviewed our response time may vary. If you agree to be considered for other roles this may result in a delayed response as we establish the best fit for your experience. We appreciate your patience. We value potential as much as experience**.** If you believe you can succeed in this role, we encourage you to apply, even if you don’t meet every requirement. We're dedicated to diversity and inclusion at EirGrid. We recognise the strength that comes from having a diverse workforce and the importance of a supportive culture for all our people to achieve their potential. Our diversity and inclusion networks have been designed to create an inclusive culture that enables our people to feel a sense of belonging at work. As an equal opportunities’ employer, we welcome applications from people of all backgrounds. Reasonable accommodations are offered at every stage of our recruitment process.

title: Inbound Specialist
description: **DON’T BE IN THE DARK ABOUT YOUR CAREER…….** ‘ARE YOU 'CUSTOMER FOCUSED \& TEAM PLAYER' WE LIKE THAT ABOUT YOU Sandvik is a global, high\-tech engineering group, providing front\-line products and services for the mining and construction industries. The Sandvik Group has been in operation since 1862 and today employs some 40,000 people, working in approximately 160 countries. Our diverse solutions delivered by our highly qualified and proficient employees enhance our customers’ productivity, safety and their profitability. **We are now recruiting for two Inbound** **Specialists \- for** **permanent roles in our Dublin Office.** **JOB PURPOSE** You manage and improve relationships with the suppliers in your portfolio and ensure agreed performance targets are met, including on\-time delivery and lead\-time accuracy. You proactively engage with suppliers to deliver improvement initiatives focused on availability and inventory reductions. You also conduct regular business review meetings and agree corrective actions if required. You ensure that the operational buying tasks are performed in a timely manner. End to end responsibility including PO placement, PO tracking from order to receipt, expediting urgent orders, resolving invoice discrepancies, data integrity maintenance and responding to sales region queries. You are also responsible for system parameter maintenance including lead\-time, safety stock levels and classification. You improve availability and reduce lead\-time through accurate and timely forecasting. **MAIN RESPONSIBILITIES** **Key performance areas** * Day to day management of operational buying activities and driving supplier performance for the respective supplier base with focus on parts availability and working capital. * Expectations focus on making and implementing sound business decisions based on available data. * High level of collaboration with internal stakeholders including: Sourcing, Engineering and Customer Service. **Key Activities** * Manage and improve relationships with the suppliers in your portfolio * Ensure agreed performance targets are met including on\-time delivery and lead\-time accuracy * Proactively engage with suppliers to deliver improvement initiatives focused on availability and inventory reductions * Conduct regular business review meetings and agree corrective actions if required * Ensure that the operational buying tasks are performed in a timely manner. End to end responsibility including PO placement, PO tracking from order to receipt, expediting urgent orders, resolving invoice discrepancies, data integrity maintenance and responding to sales region queries. * Responsible for system parameter maintenance including lead\-time, safety stock levels and classification. Improve availability and reduce lead\-time through accurate and timely forecasting. * Design and implement inventory reduction initiatives such as Vendor Managed Inventory * Support supply chain improvement projects and work closely with other departments as needed. * Understand and improve customer experience by driving improvements in the order fulfilment rate. * Ensure that the supplier portfolio is optimized: eliminate dormant suppliers, rationalize the active supply base * Other ad hoc duties as required by management **Experience** You have extensive experience in a related supply chain role and have strong knowledge of Logistics Systems and Supply Chain Management. You will also have a deep understanding of purchasing, planning and inventory control. Strong experience of supplier management and/or inventory planning is a must, as is strong knowledge of ERP systems and business management tools. You speak fluent English (another European language would be of benefit) and have the ability to travel within the region. General knowledge of Aftermarket business is an advantage. **Education, Licenses \& Certificates** * A degree level or equivalent, ideally in a business or supply chain discipline. **What we offer:** * 22 days annual leave * 4 Company Days * Defined Contribution Pension * Onsite gym * VHI Health Insurance * Life \& Disability Insurance * Health \& Wellbeing Initiatives – onsite massage therapist, gym membership, wellbeing seminars, bike to work scheme, etc * Flexible start \& Finish times * Hybrid working **Location**: Dublin, Ireland (Dockline, Mayor Street, IFSC, Dublin 1\) **Reporting to**: Purchasing Manager **Diversity, Inclusion \& Sustainability** Sandvik has a developed belief in conducting our business in a sustainable and responsible manner, both for our employees and the environment; wherever in the world we operate. title: Inbound Transport Coordinator
description: **About Keelings** Keelings is a 100% Irish\-owned family business passionate about fresh produce. Operating across Ireland, the UK, and Europe, we grow, source, and supply top\-quality fruit and vegetables. Our success is built on innovation, sustainability, and strong partnerships \- powered by our dedicated people. **Purpose of the Role** Responsible for the day\-to\-day coordination and execution of inbound transport operations across multiple international supply routes and transport modes. Operating within a fast\-paced environment, the role supports the efficient, compliant and cost\-effective movement of products from global suppliers through to the business distribution network. The successful candidate will work closely with suppliers, hauliers, customs agents, shipping partners and internal departments to ensure inbound deliveries are planned, tracked and managed effectively. The role requires strong organisational skills, attention to detail and the ability to respond quickly to operational issues in order to maintain continuity of supply and service standards. This position plays an important role in supporting inbound transport operations, monitoring logistics performance, maintaining accurate records and ensuring compliance with customs, legal and company requirements across Ireland, the UK, EU and global import markets. The role will contribute to a culture of teamwork, accountability and continuous improvement while building strong working relationships with logistics partners and internal stakeholders. **Main Responsibilities** **Inbound Logistics Coordination** * Coordinate daily inbound logistics activities across road, sea and air freight operations. * Support the planning and scheduling of inbound deliveries in line with operational requirements and product shelf\-life considerations. * Monitor inbound shipments and proactively communicate delays, disruptions or service issues to relevant stakeholders. * Liaise with suppliers, hauliers, shipping lines and freight forwarders to ensure timely collection and delivery of products. * Assist with container scheduling, haulage planning and transport coordination to support daily operational targets. * Track supplier delivery performance and escalate issues where necessary. * Support customs clearance activities by coordinating documentation with customs agents and logistics partners. * Ensure all freight documentation, transport records and logistics systems are accurately updated within required timelines. * Assist with coordinating global imports by air and sea, ensuring all required documentation is completed correctly. * Maintain strong working relationships with third\-party logistics providers and transport partners. * Support the monitoring of inbound transport costs and assist with cost control initiatives. **Operational Support** * Work closely with the Inbound Transport Manager / Supply Chain Director and wider logistics team to support smooth day\-to\-day operations. * Collaborate with Procurement, Sales, Warehouse, Technical and Planning teams to ensure efficient supply chain coordination. * Act as a key point of contact for inbound transport queries and operational updates. * Support logistics planning activities during peak trading periods and seasonal demand fluctuations. * Assist in identifying opportunities for process improvements and operational efficiencies. * Ensure all logistics activities are carried out in accordance with company Health \& Safety standards and operational procedures. * Support onboarding and training activities for new team members where required. **Compliance \& Administration** * Ensure compliance with customs procedures, transport regulations and approved haulier policies. * Assist in monitoring customs entry accuracy and resolving customs\-related queries. * Maintain accurate operational records and support KPI reporting for inbound logistics activities. * Complete daily and weekly administration tasks including shipment tracking, reporting and system updates. * Assist with invoice queries, transport cost reconciliations and supplier documentation checks. * Support supplier performance monitoring through KPI tracking and service reviews. * Ensure all required compliance and training activities are completed within designated timelines. **Key Accountabilities** * Coordination of inbound deliveries to ensure products arrive on time and in full. * Accurate maintenance of transport records, shipment documentation and logistics systems. * Effective communication with suppliers, hauliers, customs agents and internal stakeholders. * Support delivery of inbound transport service levels and operational targets. * Timely escalation and resolution of inbound logistics issues. * Maintenance of customs compliance and transport administration standards. * Contribution to continuous improvement initiatives within the logistics function. * Delivery of accurate reporting and operational support to the Inbound Transport Manager / Supply Chain Director. **Skills \& Experience** * Previous experience in logistics, transport coordination or supply chain administration, ideally within fresh produce, FMCG or a fast\-paced environment. * Understanding of international freight operations across road, sea and air freight. * Working knowledge of customs procedures and import/export documentation would be advantageous. * Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities effectively. * Excellent attention to detail and problem\-solving ability. * Strong communication and stakeholder management skills. * Ability to work effectively in a fast\-paced and constantly changing environment. * Good commercial awareness and understanding of logistics operations. * Strong IT and systems skills, including Microsoft Office and transport/logistics systems. * Ability to work collaboratively as part of a team. **Special Circumstances** * Flexibility may be required to work outside normal business hours in line with operational requirements. * Participation in an on\-call rota required to support operational issues. * Role operates on a 5 days over 7 working pattern. * Occasional travel may be required depending on operational needs. **Education \& Qualifications** * Degree, diploma or relevant experience in Supply Chain, Logistics, Business or a related discipline. * Experience in transport coordination, logistics administration or supply chain operations. * Knowledge of customs clearance processes and Incoterms would be advantageous. * Understanding of inbound logistics and international freight operations preferred. **Benefits:** * Staff discount at Keelings Farm Shop and subsidized canteen * Free On\-site parking with access to electric chargers * Wellness Initiatives such as wellness week **Keelings Values** * People Matter * Teamwork * Integrity * Passion for Achievement **Inclusion \& Diversity Statement** **In Keelings we value all perspectives and knowledge that our colleagues bring to our business. We are committed to forming a diverse and inclusive team where all can share their unique experiences and perspectives. We welcome applications from all cultures and backgrounds and are dedicated to forming an environment of inclusiveness and diversity where all can thoroughly feel a sense of belonging.** **Reasonable Accommodation Statement** **If you require reasonable accommodation during the recruitment process, please let us know at** recruitment\_keelings@keelings.com **Recruitment Process** * 1\. CV Submission \& email acknowledgment of receipt * 2\. Phone Screening * 3\. 1st Interview * 4\. 2nd Interview * 5\. Outcome \& Feedback *Keelings Careers \-* *https://www.keelings.com/careers\-keelings/* *Keelings Strategy \& Values \-* *https://www.keelings.com/our\-people/*

title: InfoSec Analyst I
description: MongoDB’s Security Assurance team is on a mission to make MongoDB one of the world’s most trusted technology vendors. We are doing this in two ways. First, we provide customers with the clear, concise and accurate information about the security of our products that customers require to use our products. Second, we serve as trusted security SMEs to MongoDB’s field teams. The InfoSec Analyst I is an independent contributor role. This role is vital to the Security Assurance team’s mission and is responsible for coordinating completion of customer due diligence reviews as well as helping MongoDB’s field personnel answer complex customer security questions. We are looking to speak to candidates who are based in Dublin for our hybrid working model. Responsibilities: * Actively manage the Assurance team’s queue to ensure requests are handled promptly * Complete customer security reviews. Help other team members complete reviews as necessary * Help customer facing personnel respond to customer security questions * Monitor relevant channels and answer in scope questions when possible, or escalate to more senior team members * Proactively maintain the team’s knowledgebase * Proactively recommend improvements in team processes and tooling to team leadership * Share relevant and actionable customer feedback with team leadership * Help coordinate development of customer facing response to high\-profile supply chain events including critical severity CVEs that may affect MongoDB or its vendors * Lead complex projects to help build, scale and train the team * Serve as an internal subject matter expert on MongoDB product security topics, assurance trends, customer concerns, and emerging regulations * Design and deliver internal enablement training on security posture and compliance topics for teams across the organization Requirements: * 2 to 3 years of experience in Information Security * BS in Computer Science, Information Systems, or equivalent experience * Direct exposure to at least one major framework or regulation such as ISO 27001, SOC2, CSA or DORA is preferred * Basic understanding of public cloud technologies including IAM, encryption, network security and tenant isolation * Strong communication skills, with the ability to present security concepts to technical and non\-technical stakeholders up to and including at the executive levels * Ability to coordinate and manage multiple urgent requests simultaneously while effectively collaborating with SMEs across various time zones * A self\-starter with a strong desire to grow into a trusted authority on the security of MongoDB and its products Success in this Role Means: * Within 3 months \- Have a solid understanding of MongoDB’s product portfolio, core security posture, and the Security Assurance team’s procedures and tooling; reliably triage incoming requests; independently complete customer security reviews with guidance from more senior team members; monitor key internal channels and answer straightforward in‑scope questions * Within 6 months \- Independently own end‑to‑end completion of most standard customer security reviews; serve as a trusted partner to customer‑facing teams by helping them respond to complex security questions; make measurable contributions to the team knowledgebase and playbooks; proactively identify and help implement improvements to team processes and tooling * Within 12 months \- Be recognized as a trusted subject matter expert on MongoDB’s product portfolio, security posture and common customer concerns; complete complex projects that materially improve review quality, throughput, or customer experience; consistently surface relevant, actionable customer feedback that can influence Security roadmaps **About MongoDB** MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the data platformbase for the AI era, enabling buildersinnovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. MongoDB’s unified database platform, the most widely available, globally distributed data platformbase on the market, helps organizations modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our cloud\-native platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi\-cloud data platformbase and is available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure. With offices worldwide and over 670,000 customers, including 75% of the Fortune 100 and AI\-native startups, relying on MongoDB for their most important applications, we’re powering the next era of software. Our compass at MongoDB is our Leadership Commitment, guiding how and why we make decisions, show up for each other, and win. It’s what makes us MongoDB. To drive the personal growth and business impact of our employees, we’re committed to developing a supportive and enriching culture for everyone. From employee affinity groups, to fertility assistance and a generous parental leave policy, we value our employees’ wellbeing and want to support them along every step of their professional and personal journeys. Learn more about what it’s like to work at MongoDB, and help us make an impact on the world! MongoDB is committed to providing any necessary accommodations for individuals with disabilities within our application and interview process. To request an accommodation due to a disability, please inform your recruiter. MongoDB is an equal opportunities employer. *Req ID: 2273456141*

title: Information Technology Cross-Function Assistant Vice President
description: 4 Jun 2026 Dublin, D, IE, D02 KF20 **Who we are** -------------- At SMBC Group EMEA, we are united by a shared purpose: to be a trusted long‑term partner to our clients. As part of our expanding EMEA platform, we provide a full suite of corporate finance, investment banking, advisory, and global markets services. With award‑winning products and a universal banking model, we offer opportunities to work on impactful, diverse, and strategic initiatives that support our clients and the bank’s long‑term transformation. Sumitomo Mitsui Finance Dublin Limited (SMFD) which is a wholly owned subsidiary of SMBC and is growing rapidly as a Centre of Excellence for the bank’s universal banking business across EMEA. It provides a range of high\-quality technology and operational and control support services, aligned to SMBC’s growth, innovation, and transformation strategies. The role will be part of a dynamic, diverse team located in the heart of Dublin’s historic Georgian business district.**Role Summary** ---------------- We are seeking an IT Technical Lead for E\-Invoicing Platform (AVP) to manage the EMEA rollout for e\-invoicing platform (PAGERO) within the Information Technology, Security Department (ITSD), Cross Product Platforms group. You will lead the design, development, architectural integration and evolution, maintenance and delivery of e\-invoicing platform across SMBC EMEA entities. This is a unique opportunity to partner with technical delivery team to implement the regulatory compliance solution, service ownership and operational excellence of integrating invoice solutions through a strategic architecture, driving transformation and influencing how SMBC supports Operations, Finance and Tax functions. The role requires strong technical expertise, and service ownership to mature this capability for EMEA region, ensuring platform stability, continuous improvement and standardisation to enhance data quality, controls and alignment with global best practices. The role also encompasses system development, major enhancements, and strategic upgrades.**Position Description** ------------------------ * Product Strategy \& Roadmap: Define and implement strategy, roadmap planning, and architectural direction for rolling out e\-invoice services across SMBC EMEA entities. * Backlog Management: Own, and refine the product backlog, prioritise technical and customer facing requirements. * Service Ownership: Owning the end‑to‑end delivery lifecycle covering solution design, development oversight, system testing, and controlled implementation of e\-invoicing platform. * System Integration: Enable the integration of e\-invoicing platform – PAGERO, with multiple invoice processing systems, including COUPA, Accounts Payable (ERP System) and migrating some in\-house built invoice applications to the strategic platform. * Stakeholder Alignment: Collaborate with stakeholders across Finance, Tax, Operations, Regulatory Reporting to understand requirements, prioritise needs, and optimise invoicing solutions * Vendor Management: Manage vendor relationships, contracts and escalations effectively * Establish standardised operating models, governance controls, and quality frameworks across EMEA entities for invoice processing. * Monitor platform performance, risk, compliance, and audit findings, and leading the implementation of continuous improvements, remediation actions, and control uplifts **Required Experience** ----------------------- * Domain Knowledge: Good understanding of Order\-to\-cash (O2C) processes, including experience designing integrations between SaaS platforms and ERP / Finance systems (Accounts payable, Accounts Receivable) and billing. Ideally hands\-on experience with e\-invoicing SaaS platforms (e.g. PAGERO, COUPA etc.) * Experience operating as a product owner in technology or a change lead accountable for delivery, funded roadmaps and long\-term technology assets. * Agile Experience: Proven track record of working in agile/scrum methodologies, managing sprints and evaluating development progress with developers, QA and architects. * Delivery Management: Confident delivering at pace, across BAU, change delivery, testing cycles, and incident management * Strong communication, analytical and decision‑making skills, with a focus on structured problem‑solving and control improvement **Desirable Experience \& Education and Qualifications** -------------------------------------------------------- * Experience working in a hybrid business/technology domain * Degree‑level education in Information Technology, Computer Science, Finance, Business Information Systems or a related field (or equivalent professional experience) * Professional certifications such as ITIL, Agile/Scrum, or data management qualifications are advantageous but not essential **Competencies** ---------------- Customer Focus Driving Change Driving Results Embraces Diversity Enterprise Leadership Judgement and Decision Making Risk Management Strategic and Visionary Trust and Integrity**What we offer** ----------------- For all the expertise and experience you bring to help us make a difference, it’s only sustainable if we support you to build your career and be your best self so we offer competitive company benefits, centred around promoting positive well\-bring and work\-life balance. We also believe in fostering a diverse and inclusive work environment, where all team members perspectives and contributions are valued. Initiatives in place which promote a diverse and inclusive culture and healthy work life balance include hybrid working, Sport \& Social Clubs and Diversity and Inclusion networks. As an employee of SMBC you have access to a host of both useful and exciting benefit offerings. See below some of the benefits on offer to you: * Hybrid and flexible working * Competitive paid leave days * Benefits to support your physical wellbeing, including private medical insurance and life and invalidity insurance * Various policies to support your mental wellbeing, including a robust behavioural health network with counselling and coaching services * Access a wide range of learning and development opportunities and career progression opportunities * Ambitious remuneration package So, if you like a challenge and want to continuously grow and develop in a role where you will be supported along the way by a dynamic and diverse team, apply today!! **About SMBC** -------------- A trusted partner for the long term United by a sense of purpose towards our customers \- to be a trusted partner for the long\-term \- and our universal banking platform in EMEA, we deliver a full suite of corporate finance products and solutions to our customers, including corporate, structured and trade finance, leveraged finance, loan market and treasury products. We can also provide investment banking and advisory services and a range of innovative solutions in global capital markets. about what we do and who we are at https://www.smbcgroup.com/emea/careers

title: Information Technology Support Engineer
description: **IT Support Engineer (Windows \& Mac)** **Job Description** One of Irelands best clients to work for is looking for a Snr Windows IT Support Engineer to join their End User Computing team. This role will be responsible for providing advanced technical support across a diverse technology environment, ensuring excellent service delivery and maintaining a secure, reliable end\-user experience. **Job Responsibilities** * Provide advanced technical support for end users across Windows and Mac environments via phone, email, remote support, and ticketing platforms. * Troubleshoot and resolve complex hardware, software, operating system, and application\-related issues. * Support and administer Microsoft 365 services, Active Directory, Exchange, Azure Active Directory, and enterprise applications. * Manage user account provisioning, permissions, access controls, and group administration. * Deploy, configure, and maintain desktop, laptop, mobile, and peripheral devices throughout their lifecycle. * Perform software deployment, patch management, and endpoint administration using enterprise management tools. * Investigate system alerts, identify root causes of technical issues, and escalate where necessary. * Assist with network\-related troubleshooting across LAN and WAN environments in collaboration with infrastructure teams. * Maintain accurate technical documentation, knowledge base articles, and user training materials. * Support endpoint security initiatives, vulnerability remediation activities, and patch compliance programmes. * Participate in incident response, disaster recovery testing, and operational support activities. * Collaborate closely with infrastructure, application support, security, and global IT teams to deliver high\-quality services. * Participate in scheduled maintenance activities, including server patching and on\-call support rotations. * Contribute to the continuous improvement of IT service management processes and operational procedures. **Experience Required** * Minimum 4\-5 years' experience in an Windows IT Support, Service Desk, Desktop Support, or End User Computing environment. * Strong hands\-on experience supporting both Windows and Mac operating systems. * Experience managing device deployment, endpoint administration, and operating system lifecycle activities. * Proven experience supporting Microsoft 365, Active Directory, Azure Active Directory, and Exchange environments. * Knowledge of software deployment and patch management solutions within enterprise environments. * Understanding of networking fundamentals including DNS, DHCP, TCP/IP, VPN, and endpoint connectivity troubleshooting. * Experience working within structured IT service management frameworks. * Strong problem\-solving skills with the ability to prioritise workload effectively in a fast\-paced environment. * Excellent communication and customer service skills with a user\-focused approach. **Desirable Skills** * Experience with ITSM platforms such as ServiceNow, Jira Service Management, or similar tools. * Exposure to endpoint management and device management platforms including Intune or equivalent technologies. * Knowledge of Apple device administration and security management. * Familiarity with vulnerability management processes and endpoint security best practices. * Basic scripting or automation experience using PowerShell or similar technologies. * Experience supporting enterprise\-scale environments with global users. * Relevant industry certifications such as CompTIA A\+, ITIL Foundation, Microsoft certifications, or Apple certifications. **Educational Requirements** * Bachelor's Degree or Third Level Qualification in Information Technology, Computer title: Infrastructure Analyst
description: Role Purpose The Infrastructure Systems Administrator will play a key role in the delivery, support, security, and ongoing development of enterprise ICT infrastructure services. The successful candidate will work within a larger ICT team and contribute to the administration, maintenance, enhancement, and strategic evolution of on\-premise and cloud\-based infrastructure platforms. The role requires strong technical expertise across Microsoft cloud technologies, systems administration, virtualization, networking, security, vendor management, and ICT project delivery. Qualifications \& Experience Essential Qualifications Candidates should ideally possess: * A Level 8 qualification, as recognised on the National Framework of Qualifications (NFQ), or equivalent, in: + Computer Science + Information Technology + or another relevant discipline. Candidates holding a relevant Level 7 or Level 6 qualification will also be considered. Successful candidates appointed with these qualifications will be required to complete a Level 8 qualification (supported by the organisation) within a three\-year timeframe. Essential Experience * Minimum of 2 years' experience in an Enterprise ICT environment working as a Systems Administrator with a strong focus on Microsoft cloud services. * Demonstrated experience in: + Solution design + Solution implementation + System maintenance + Cost and billing management * 3\-5 years' hands\-on experience with relevant technologies including: + Microsoft Azure + Microsoft 365 / Office 365 + Azure Identity and Access Management + Exchange Online + Microsoft Teams + Active Directory + DHCP and DNS + VMware + Commvault * Microsoft certifications in relevant Azure and Microsoft 365 technologies. Desirable Qualifications \& Experience * Experience with comparable cloud technologies such as AWS. * Networking, firewall, web filtering, and application filtering knowledge and certifications such as: + CCNA + CCNP * ITIL v3/2011 or ITIL v4 certification. * PRINCE2 Project Management certification. * Experience with: + IPS / IDS + PAM + IAM + SIEM + Syslog technologies * Strong Microsoft Windows Server administration skills including: + Windows firewalls + Security hardening * Mobile Device Management experience using technologies such as: + Microsoft Intune + AirWatch * Public sector procurement experience. * Experience with ICT ticketing systems and service management. * Experience working within large ICT teams including: + Service Delivery + Networking + Security + Applications + Research \& Innovation Knowledge \& Skills The successful candidate will demonstrate: * Strong Systems Administration and/or Networking Administration skills. * Vendor and Managed Service Provider management experience. * Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder engagement skills. * Strong customer service orientation with the ability to anticipate and respond to changing business needs. * Procurement and supplier management capabilities. * ICT project management experience including planning, delivery, and coordination of technical projects. * Knowledge of ITIL Service Management methodology. * Strong analytical, troubleshooting, and problem\-solving skills. * Ability to work independently and collaboratively within multidisciplinary teams. * Fluent English communication skills, both written and verbal. Behavioural Competencies The successful candidate will demonstrate: * Effective decision\-making within a business context. * A structured, organised, and methodical approach to work. * Commitment to quality service delivery and continuous improvement. * Strong resource planning and organisational capability. * Positive and supportive leadership behaviours. * Understanding of organisational and operational business context. * Strong information management and documentation skills. * Commitment to teamwork and collaboration. * Flexibility and adaptability in response to changing priorities. * Initiative and proactive problem\-solving. * Resilience and ability to perform under pressure while managing competing priorities. Main Duties \& Responsibilities The successful candidate will: * Contribute as a member of the Infrastructure Team to the delivery and support of enterprise infrastructure services. * Manage and administer virtual server environments and cloud infrastructure platforms. * Support and maintain technologies including: + Windows and Linux servers + Active Directory + VMware + SAN technologies + Commvault + Veeam + File sharing platforms + Monitoring platforms + Microsoft 365 + Azure + Azure AD / Entra ID + Microsoft Teams + Dynamics 365 CRM + Dataverse + PowerApps + Network infrastructure including Juniper, Dell, and Cisco devices * Provide technical guidance and oversight to Managed Service Providers and external suppliers. * Participate in Disaster Recovery and Business Continuity planning and testing activities. * Lead and support security hardening initiatives across cloud and on\-premise systems. * Assist in the configuration and management of mobile devices through Microsoft Intune. * Support procurement activities including drafting, reviewing, and evaluating tenders for ICT services, software, and hardware. * Participate in supplier and contract management activities including risk management and performance reviews. * Track and manage ICT hardware and software assets throughout their lifecycle. * Lead planning, testing, and deployment of: + Security patches + Operating system upgrades + Software upgrades * Support rollout and maintenance of security and monitoring tools. * Manage incident resolution and participate in problem management processes. * Project manage infrastructure and cloud migration initiatives. * Contribute to infrastructure documentation and technical standards. * Research and evaluate emerging technologies and service models. * Maintain continuous professional development and participate in performance management processes. * Support organisational quality customer service commitments. * Comply fully with health, safety, and organisational policies. * Undertake additional duties as assigned. Key Technologies * Microsoft Azure * Microsoft 365 / Office 365 * Azure Entra ID * Exchange Online * Microsoft Teams * Active Directory * VMware * Commvault * Veeam * Windows Server * Linux * DHCP / DNS * Intune * Cisco / Juniper / Dell networking * Dataverse * Power Platform * Dynamics 365 Desirable Certifications * Microsoft Azure Certifications * Microsoft 365 Certifications * CCNA / CCNP * ITIL v4 * PRINCE2 * Security\-related certifications Working Relationships The role involves close collaboration with: * ICT Infrastructure Teams * Service Delivery Teams * Security Teams * Managed Service Providers * Software and Hardware Vendors * Internal Business Stakeholders

title: Installation Helper
description: **Installation Helper – Industrial Doors** **About The Role** We are seeking an Installation helper to join our team. title: Internship Opportunities 2026
description: **At Cadence, we hire and develop leaders and innovators who want to make an impact on the world of technology.** Cadence is a pivotal leader in electronic design, building upon more than 30 years of computational software expertise. The company applies its underlying Intelligent System Design strategy to deliver software, hardware and IP that turn design concepts into reality. Cadence customers are the world’s most innovative companies, delivering extraordinary electronic products from chips to boards to systems for the most dynamic market applications including consumer, hyperscale computing, 5G communications, automotive, aerospace industrial and health. Cadence also offer everything else you would expect from a world\-class technology organization and a Fortune 100 company, which is ranked as one of the best places to work worldwide: https://fortune.com/best\-companies/2020/cadence Cadence Cork are recruiting key talent across several Universities to join our Internship Programme for 2026\. If you are passionate about technology and would love to learn and grow in a cross\-geographic and multi\-cultural organization, developing next\-generation software, then Cadence Cork is for you. As part of your experience with Cadence, you will join a new class of professionals in our Career Catalyst Program. Through this program, you’ll receive special opportunities to develop your leadership capabilities, learn from Cadence executives and other industry leaders, and socialize and network with your peers. ***At Cadence, we hire and develop leaders and innovators who want to make an impact on the world of technology.*** **Job Title: Intern Opportunities 2026 (Software / Hardware Design)** **Location: Cork/Galway/Dublin** **Job Overview** We aim to make you an integral part of the team by providing training, mentorship, and encouragement of your creative talents. Our goal is to equip students with practical work experience. We understand that it is best to learn by doing. We will provide tasks that enable you to learn while making meaningful technical contributions. As part of the Cadence Internship program, you will get the opportunity to support different Business Units across the following functions including; * Digital Design Signoff * Customer IC / Analog / RF Design * System Analysis – including Thermal Design Management, EM solvers ad CFD Analysis * PCB Design and Analysis * System Design and Verification **The wealth of knowledge, experience, learning and autonomous culture of the team makes it a great team to work with.** **Job Qualifications** * Currently enrolled as BS or MS or Phd student majoring in Mathematics, Physics, Computer Science / Electrical / Electronic Engineering / Digital Humanities \& Information Technology or similar. * Looking for strong C\+\+, algorithms, and software development / scripting skills. * Understanding of analog/RF/custom IC design and verification practices is a strong plus. * Exposure to Cadence Virtuoso Layout and physical verification tools is a plus. * Team player, driven, self\-motivated and autonomous. * Excellent communication, presentation and customer service skills. * Problem Solving skills. **Additional Information** Cadence is committed to equal employment opportunity and employment equity throughout all levels of the organization. We strive to attract a qualified and diverse candidate pool and encourage diversity and inclusion in the workplace. **We’re doing work that matters. Help us solve what others can’t.**

title: Junior Account Executive
description: We are looking for an **Account Executive for BBH** , one of the most prestigious creative agencies in the world. They believe in the power of difference, in challenging norms, and sparking change in brands and businesses. With a rich continuous success since their founding 1981, they've crafted memorable campaigns that evoke laughter, tears, and admiration. Now expanding to their eighth office in Dublin, they seek innovative minds to continue their impactful legacy. Account Executives (AEs) are the foundations of account management teams. They are the glue. As an AE you are the person the team looks to to get stuff sorted. To get everyone in the right place at the right time. AE’s know what is going on and are always one step ahead. If that sounds like you, keep reading! **What’s in it for you?** Excellent Salary \| Amazing culture \| Career progression \| Hybrid work \| Learn from the best **Responsibilities:** * Practice front\-foot account management, planning ahead, knowing your timings inside out. * knowing the team’s movements and planning around these commitments to ensure smooth project delivery * Understand the day to day finances on your account and be responsible for keeping project finance trackers \& timesheets up to date * Deliver all tasks on the deadline OR flag where this is not possible to line managers in advance. * Articulate the different brand positions for your client’s key competitors. * Develop strong relationships with all client contacts. * Passionately care about great creative work. Identifying great work and articulating why. * Provide inspiration during creative briefings with relevant references. * Support new business pitch teams with a can\-do and proactive attitude. * Ensure all meetings run smoothly, booking rooms, preparing the environment \& materials, and confirming attendees and roles in advance. * Be curious and constantly seeking to learn. **Requirements:** * You have a background in Marketing or similar degree. * Minimum of 1 year of experience in account management, ideally in a creative agency. * You are passionate about creativity and building long\-lasting relationships. * Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills, with a knack for building and nurturing client relationships * You are keen to take bold leaps, even if that means you risk making mistakes along the way. * Highly organized and detail\-oriented, adept at multitasking and time management. * You are voraciously curious and never stop feeding your mind * Self\-motivated and proactive, capable of both independent work and collaboration within a team. If you would like to work with one of the best creative agencies out there, learn from the best and progress in your career, this is a big opportunity for you! We can’t wait to meet you, apply through Prosperity now!

title: Junior Consultant - Launch Your Sales Career
description: * **Unlimited commission \- the more you deliver, the more you earn** * **Fast promotions \- average performers get promoted every 6 months** * **Meritocracy \- A career that grows as quickly as you do** **Access** is a healthcare recruitment agency based in central **Dublin** . We are currently looking for professionals to join us as **Recruitment Consultants** , supporting our mission to become the \#1 healthcare recruitment agency in Ireland while expanding into international markets. If you are: * Someone who’s consistently performed and **progressed in your career** * **Commercially sharp** \- you spot opportunities and act on them * Competitive, driven, and motivated by **commission and growth** And you are looking for: * **Unlimited commission** with strong earning potential from day one * A clear, structured path into **Senior and Leadership roles** * **Hands\-on training** and daily support from top performers and managers * A high\-energy, **merit\-based environment** where results are recognised **This is for you!** **A Day in the Life of a Recruitment Consultant** No two days are the same. Mornings start with coffee and a quick team huddle to set goals, then it’s straight into **live search assignments, writing standout job ads, and keeping multiple processes moving at pace.** The rest of the day is about **people, momentum, and results** \- meeting ambitious candidates, partnering with clients, prepping talent for interviews, and celebrating wins as deals come together.
