title: National Account Executive - Dublin - 13-15 Month FTC
description: **Company Description** **Who we are?** We are JDE Peet’s. We craft coffee for every cup and build brands for every heart. JDE Peet’s is the world’s leading pure\-play coffee company, serving approximately 4,400 cups of coffee per second in more than 100 markets. Guided by our ‘Reignite the Amazing’ strategy, we are focusing on brand\-led growth across three big bets: Peet’s, L’OR, and Jacobs, alongside a collection of 9 local icons; such as Douwe Egberts, Kenco, Pilao, Old Town, Moccona, Super, Gevalia, Friele, Marcilla. In 2024, JDE Peet’s generated total sales of EUR 8\.8 billion and employed a global workforce of more than 21,000 employees. **What’s it like to work at JDE Peet’s?** We believe amazing things happen over a cup of coffee. Together, we create those moments every day, turning ideas into impact and possibilities into reality. At JDE Peet’s, we work as one team guided by our values: we Dare to Amaze, Own It, Make It Simple, and Win Together. Here, you’ll find the freedom to innovate, the courage to take bold steps, and the support to grow your career in a global environment. Come join our team and discover what your cup of amazing can be! Find out more at http://careers.jdepeets.com **Job Description** The National Account Executive supports the management and growth of key national customers across Ireland. The role focuses on supporting Account Managers, executing customer plans, analysing sales data, and ensuring excellence in promotional admin execution. This role is an incredible opportunity for anyone who has an interest in the FMCG industry. The successful candidate will have the chance to develop, improve, and hone highly transferable commercial skills applicable across industries and markets. Additional to the National Account Executive responsibilities, our candidate will have the opportunity to assist marketing and category management, gaining firsthand knowledge of these exciting roles. **Key Responsibilities** * Support the National Account managers with their day\-today tasks, including new product set up, running reports, creating presentations etc. * Prepare and submit promotional submissions in line with the customer calendars. * Create promotional briefs to share with the field sales team. * Manage promotional funding and forecasting in our internal platform XTEL. * Monitor customer pricing and amend any discrepancies in SAP. * Support Marketing with PR activity and budget management. * Assist Category Management with data analysis and creating presentations. **Qualifications** Working at JDE Peet’s is hands\-on, giving you the chance to leave your mark while working together as one team. We’re a fast\-paced company on a mission, focused on what matters most. Our environment demands energy, agility, and bold thinking. If you’re ready to make an impact and grow in a global team, JDE Peet’s is the place for you. For this role as a National Account Executive, we’re looking for someone who has the following skills and experience: * 1\-3 years’ experience in sales or a commercial role (FMCG experience highly valued). * Proficient with Microsoft Office programmes, particularly Excel. * Strong numerical and analytical skills with high attention to detail. * Excellent communication and presentation skills. * Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑paced commercial environment. * Full right to work/work permits for the duration of the contract **Additional Information** **Your application at JDE Peet's** If you are interested in this career opportunity at JDE Peet's, apply and join us. After receiving your application we will do our utmost to give you quick feedback. Part of the recruitment procedure at JDE Peet's would be an online assessment, followed by interviews. A pre\-employment check can be part of the recruitment process. **Our DE\&I Statement** At JDE Peet’s we aim to create a better future where we authentically serve, reflect and embrace everyone. We believe in fostering a more diverse, equitable and inclusive organisation where everyone feels comfortable to truly be who they are and unleash their full potential. This means we do not tolerate discrimination on the basis of our race, ethnicity, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, religion, nationality, age, disability, or any other trait protected by law. Throughout the entire employee lifecycle, we place DE\&I at the core of our people processes. This enables us to drive objectivity and eliminate any potential bias, ensuring an inclusive experience in the workplace. Do not hesitate to reach out if you require any reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process. **Our efforts on this agenda have earned us the prestigious Catalyst Award, as well as the Fair Pay Leader Certification in 2025 thanks to an unexplained gender pay gap of 0\.52%.** *\#unleashyourpossibility \#acupofamazing* JDE Peet’s \- now part of Keurig Dr Pepper – is the world’s leading pure\-play coffee company, with a presence in more than 100 markets. Guided by our ‘Reignite the Amazing’ strategy, we are focused on brand\-led growth across three big bets: Peet’s, L’OR, and our 10 strategically selected local icons led by Jacobs.

title: Outbound Sales Representative
description: **Outbound Sales Representative** Grand Canal Quay, Dublin, Ireland Full\-time / Hybrid ***Who We Are:*** Irishjobs is a leading online job board in Ireland dedicated to connecting job seekers with their ideal employment opportunities. As a major player in the Irish job market, we had a key role in facilitating millions of job placements across various sectors and industries. We are proud to be part of The Stepstone Group, a global expert in job\-tech platforms. Being part of a global team allows us to leverage cutting\-edge technology, data, and industry expertise to enhance our services and continuously innovate our technology to meet the needs of the job market. ***The Job at a Glance:*** IrishJobs.ie (part of The Stepstone Group) is looking to hire an Outbound Sales Representative for their Dublin office. Join our team if you’re the kind of person who thrives on high activity, loves getting decision\-makers on the phone, and wants a role where hard work directly turns into excellent earnings and progression — this one’s for you. This is an **outbound\-first** sales role where the expectation is clear: **80–100 dials per day** (high call volume, phone\-led selling). If that excites you (not scares you), you’ll have a genuine opportunity to build a career quickly here — including a clear pathway into an Account Manager role after 12 months, where you’ll combine new business with managing and growing your own customer base. **Progression: fast\-track your sales career (12\-month pathway)** This role is designed to accelerate your career if you perform: * Year 1: Outbound\-first full\-cycle new business (build your wins, sharpen your sales habits) * After 12 months: move into Account Manager scope — blending new business with managing and renewing your own accounts. ***In this role you will:*** * Prospect and call B2B businesses daily (targeting high outbound activity; phone\-first approach). * Run discovery conversations to understand hiring needs and position IrishJobs solutions. * Pitch, quote, and close deals — and own the customers you win * Build your pipeline, track activity, and work towards clear monthly targets with visibility on earnings. ***Your Skills and Experience:*** We’re focused on mindset \+ activity \+ resilience — not fancy titles. You’ll likely be a great fit if you have: * Experience in **high\-volume outbound sales** essential (80–100 calls/day is ideal). * The confidence to pick up the phone, handle objections, and keep going. * A target\-driven mentality and genuine motivation to earn well. * B2B sales, inside sales, recruitment agency, insurance/telco outbound, or call\-centre style sales backgrounds can all work brilliantly here. ***Your Benefits:*** We’re a community here that cares as much about your life outside work as how you feel when you’re with us. Because your job shouldn’t take over your life, it should enrich it. Here are some of the benefits we offer: * 23 days holiday allowance \+ 2 company days \+ bank holidays * After each year in the company, you get an extra day (up to 4 days) * Private medical healthcare * Pension contribution up to 5% * Wellhub – all in one platform for gyms, nutrition, and overall wellbeing * Enhanced payment terms for various family leave types, including Maternity, Paternity, and Adoption. * 24/7 Employee Assistance Programme * Life Assurance Cover * Bike to work scheme * Educational assistance * Hybrid working model * Volunteering days ***Our commitment*** Equal opportunities are important to us. We believe that diversity and inclusion at The Stepstone Group are critical to our success as a global company, so we want to recruit, develop, and keep the best talent. We encourage applications from everyone, regardless of background, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability status, ethnicity, belief, age, family or parental status, and any other characteristic.

title: Packaging Operative
description: **Packaging Operative \- Avoca Central Bakery \& Kitchen Bray** Avoca operates in 13 locations across the country. After all this time, the ethos of Avoca remains the same. We cherish our time\-honoured traditions, which have been lovingly passed down through several generations. Our skills might be steeped in a long and rich tradition, but our attitude is to look to the future. We are now heralded as one of Ireland's most exciting retail stores, with a host of award\-winning cafes, restaurants and food markets crammed with artisanal ingredients from near and far. **Job Summary:** The Packing Operative will be responsible for the day\-to\-day function of the CBK packaging activity. This role is a highly focused on food packaging. A Food Packaging Operative prepares, packs, and labels food products on production, ensuring quality standards and safety regulations (HACCP) are met. Responsibilities include operating machinery, inspecting products for defects, maintaining workstation hygiene, and working efficiently in fast\-paced, between ambient and chilled environments. Requires attention to detail, teamwork, and manual dexterity. **Responsibilities:** * Efficiently pack, seal, and label food products, often at high speeds. * Inspect items for defects, damage, or contamination before packaging. * Set up, operate, and make minor adjustments to packing machinery. * Adhere to strict health, safety, and hygiene standards (PPE required, such as hairnets, bear snood, gloves, coats, safety shoes). * Keep work areas and machinery clean and sanitized. * Complete daily paperwork, including packaging and labelling records. * Move packed goods to racks, dispatch crates or storage areas. **Requirements:** * Attention to Detail. Ability to spot quality issues and follow exact specifications. * Ability to stand for long periods and perform repetitive manual tasks. * Work effectively with colleagues and report faults to supervisors. * Basic skills for reading instructions, labelling, and checking quantities. * Strong work ethic with a focus on punctuality and attendance. * Requires no formal experience (entry\-level), with on\-the\-job training provided. * Previous experience in manufacturing, food, or warehouse roles is advantageous Ability to work in a fast\-paced environment to meet deadlines **Benefits:** * Staff Discount * Employee Assistance Programme * Day time hours Monday \- Friday 9am\-6pm * Cycle to Work Scheme * Pension Scheme

title: PhD Studentship
description: Trinity is Ireland’s leading university and is ranked 75th in the world (QS World University Rankings 2026\). Founded in 1592, the University is steeped in history with a reputation for excellence in education, research, and innovation, which has been inspiring generations of thinkers for over 400 years. Thrive: Trinity College Dublin's Strategic Plan 2025 \- 2030 The renovation and public closure of Trinity College Dublin’s (TCD) Long Room in the Old Library present an unparalleled opportunity to conduct pioneering research “on”, “through”, and “for” this iconic space. This initiative will redefine how we preserve and enhance one of Europe’s premier cultural heritage landmarks. This project will delve into aspects of the Long Room that have been largely overlooked: its acoustic experiences and sonic architecture. This project will use state\-of\-the\-art robot\-aided acoustic measurements and employ advanced computational and acoustic modelling techniques to create a versatile Acoustic Digital Twin, which will be combined with existing 3D image data. **Application Procedure** Application Deadline: 12pm (GMT), 26 June 2026 Applicants should submit a completed application package to: Name: Jennifer O’Meara, Associate Professor in Film Studies Email Address: jennifer.omeara@tcd.ie **Our Values \- putting our words into action** Values matter. They are key to the success of our Strategic Plan. We centre our behavioural values around the importance of *belonging*. We recognise that the university will thrive by each of us *respecting and caring* for one another. We know we can *achieve* much more through working *together*. We know too that we must *take responsibility* for our actions and be accountable. We must, and will never stop, *imagining*. And through *being nature positive* we connect not just with the environment but with our ambitions for a sustainable future. At Trinity, we are committed to equality, diversity, and inclusion. Trinity welcomes applications from all individuals, including those applicants with disabilities, those who may have had non\-traditional career paths, those who have taken time out for reasons including family or caring responsibilities. We also welcome international applicants including those who have been displaced due to war. Trinity College Dublin is Ireland’s premier University. We are an EU Sustainable Gender Equality Champion and we hold an Athena Swan Silver award, recognising our ongoing work to advance gender equality both within Trinity and in the Higher Education sector. Trinity is committed to supporting work\-life balance and to creating a family\-friendly working environment.

title: Pharmacy Over the Counter Assistant
description: **City Pharmacy** , an independent and fully Irish\-owned pharmacy is looking to recruit an **Pharmacy OTC Assistant** with excellent conditions to join the team. This is a permanent position available on a **full\-time or part\-time basis** , depending on the availability of the right candidate. Therefore you will work **5 days per week (Monday to Saturday)** . No Sundays, late nights, or bank holidays are required. The successful candidate will be responsible for the smooth running of the front\-of\-shop, attending to customers, selling over\-the\-counter medicines and cosmetics, ordering stock, and managing the till. We are looking for someone who is **professional** , **caring** , and **enthusiastic** and will provide the best care to customers at all times. **Requirements** * Minimum experience of 1 year in a similar community pharmacy OTC role * Must have strong product knowledge of OTC medical products and cosmetics * Proven experience in medicine sales, merchandising, and stock control * Strong customer service skills * Attention to detail and organizational skills * Process orders \& deal with reps. * Keep shop floor tidy, clean, and well\-maintained. * Supporting Pharmacists to provide guidance and expert advice to other pharmacy staff about the safe use of medicines, particularly OTC and pharmacy\-only medicines **Key Candidate Attributes** * Approachable, friendly, and professional *We are seeking an individual who naturally exudes approachability and professionalism, creating an environment where customers feel welcome and valued.* * Excellent customer service and communication skills *Exceptional communication skills are a non\-negotiable requirement. The candidate should excel in engaging with customers, ensuring their needs are met proficiently, and contributing to a positive shopping experience.* * Bright, hard\-working, and takes pride in their work. *Our ideal candidate is characterized by a bright and industrious demeanor, taking genuine pride in their contributions, and consistently striving for excellence.* * A positive and enthusiastic outlook, *A positive outlook and enthusiastic approach are indispensable attributes that will not only uplift team spirits but also radiate to our valued customers.* * The ability to show initiative and to work well as part of a team. *The candidate should possess a proactive mindset, showcasing the ability to take initiative and creatively contribute to the overall success of the team.* **What we offer:** * Staff Discounts * Yearly Bonus * Tax Saver Scheme * Heath Insurance Contribution * Pension Scheme Contribution * Bike\-to\-work * Competitive Salary **Benefits:** * Bike to work scheme * Company pension * Employee discount * Private medical insurance * Store discount

title: Pipe Fitter Apprentice Year 1
description: **The Role** * Due to continuous growth within the company, Murphy Ireland are currently looking for Apprentice Pipefitter(s) to join and contribute towards some new and exciting projects. * Please note that travel is required **Key Responsibilities** * Assist in the fabrication, installation, maintenance, and repair of high\- and low\-pressure piping systems. * Support experienced pipefitters in measuring, cutting, prepping, threading, and assembly of pipe fabrications of varying sizes (from small\-bore to large\-diameter systems). * Help install piping systems that carry liquids, gases, and other materials in accordance with technical drawings and specifications. * Participate in welding, fabrication, and fitting activities under supervision. * Learn to interpret and follow engineering drawings, fabrication drawings, and isometric diagrams. * Use and maintain a range of hand tools, power tools, and specialised pipefitting equipment safely and effectively. * Assist in testing, inspecting, and troubleshooting piping systems to identify faults or leaks. * Support routine preventative maintenance activities to ensure system reliability and safety. * Maintain a high level of Health and Safety which includes the proper use of PPE. * Adhere to work instructions and procedures. * Meet daily, weekly and monthly targets. * Keep site and fabshop clean and safe to operate in. **Key Requirements** **Qualifications \& Skills:** - Must be eligible to register as a pipefitting apprentice with SOLAS - The minimum educational requirements are: * Grade D in five subjects in the Department of Education \& Skills Junior Certificate Examination or an approved equivalent, or 2\. * The successful completion of an approved Pre\-Apprenticeship course or 3\. * Three years’ work experience gained over sixteen years of age in a relevant designated industrial activity as SOLAS shall deem acceptable. * Safe Pass, Manual Handling and Abrasive Wheels * Leaving Certificate with O6 – O1 in Maths (desirable but not required). Physics and technical drawing is beneficial * Previous construction experience is beneficial * Any additional tickets/training is beneficial * Full Driving Licence is required * Understanding Health and Safety compliance **What Murphy Ireland Offer In Return** Murphy Ireland pride themselves in creating opportunities to work with a great team on some of Irelands largest and most exciting construction projects. **Along With Great Opportunities/career Progression, Murphy Ireland Also Offer** * MEBSCA Rates * 21 days annual leave per annum * Large pipeline of work * Dedicated and continued investment in your professional development. * Other Murphy benefits include retail discounts, cycle to work scheme etc **Schedule** * Monday to Friday, weekend work if required * Work Location: In person both in Newbridge or on site as required by line manager **About Murphy** Operating in Ireland, the United Kingdom, Canada and the USA, Murphy provides better engineered solutions to infrastructure sectors including transportation, water, power and natural resources. So that our teams out on projects can run smoothly it is vital that we have robust support functions in place. We are committed to fostering an engaging and collaborative work environment in which each person’s career is nurtured and developed. We focus on employee development, providing a culture of innovation and positive work\-life balance. Murphy is formally recognised as a sector leading employer, Platinum Investors In People accredited. At Murphy, we are incredibly proud of our diverse workforce. Not only do our people represent over 60 different nationalities, but they also represent a wide range of backgrounds, ethnicities and ages. If you are unable to apply via the usual process, please call Susanne Mueller on 0873423654 or email emergingtalentireland@murphygroup.ie. Murphy is unable to employ anyone who does not have the legal right to live and work in Ireland.

title: Platform Engineer (Automation) - Unannounced Project
description: Scopely is looking for a Senior Platform Engineer(Automation) to join a new truly unique multiplayer strategy game in Spain, Ireland, Portugal or the UK on a remote or hybrid basis. We can support with visa sponsorship and relocation assistance from any location. At Scopely, we care deeply about what we do and want to inspire play every day \- whether in our work environments alongside our talented colleagues or through our deep connections with our communities of players. We are a global team of game lovers who are developing, publishing and innovating the mobile games industry, connecting millions of people around the world daily. We are in the early stages of development on an ambitious, unannounced Strategy/MMO title, creating a team of talented and passionate game makers to join us on this exciting journey! **What You’ll Do** * We are looking for a software engineer with a strong automation mindset to help improve how our engineering teams builds, tests, and deliver softwares. * This role sits at the intersection of automation, reliability, quality, and developer experience. You’ll work on the systems and tooling that enable teams to ship changes safely and repeatedly, reducing manual effort and operational risk through software. * Design, and evolve automation as internal product, to support the software delivery lifecycle, from code commit through to production * Improve the reliability, consistency, and usability of CI/CD pipelines and supporting tooling * Identify manual, error\-prone, or slow processes workflows and replace them with automation and a product\-driven approach * Collaborate with application engineers, and infrastructure teams to improve delivery safety, resilience, and operational excellence * Contribute to standards and reusable patterns for build, test, deployment, and rollback * Embed reliability, security, and quality checks into automated workflows where they add real value * Improve developer experience through better tooling, documentation, and self\-service capabilities * Use metrics and feedback to guide improvements to delivery performance and system reliability **What We’re Looking For** * Background as a software engineer, SRE, DevOps engineer, or platform engineer with a strong focus on automation * Ability to quickly navigate and understand unfamiliar codebases, proactively identifying and implementing improvements that enhance software engineering patterns (SOLID, DRY), reliability, observability, and overall system health with minimal supervision * Experience improving developer productivity or internal tooling * Experience operating systems across multiple environments (dev, test, prod) * Strong collaboration and communication skills when working across teams * Experience building or maintaining CI/CD pipelines using tools such as GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins, or similar * Comfortable writing automation and tooling in a general\-purpose language (e.g. Python, Go, or equivalent) * Working knowledge of cloud environments (AWS, GCP, or Azure), even if not a deep specialist * Familiarity with containerized workloads and modern deployment practices (ECS, Kubernetes/EKS, or similar) **Bonus Points** * Exposure to Site Reliability Engineering practices or operational ownership * Experience working with infrastructure as code tools (Terraform, CloudFormation, or similar) is a plus, not a requirement * Familiarity with delivery performance metrics (e.g. deployment frequency, lead time, failure rates) * Interest in software supply chain security or compliance automation **About Scopely** Scopely is a leading video game and global interactive entertainment company, home to many of the world’s most beloved and enduring experiences, including two of the most successful mobile games of all\-time “MONOPOLY GO!” and “Pokémon GO,” along with “Stumble Guys,” “Star Trek™ Fleet Command,” “MARVEL Strike Force,” “WWE Champions,” the Scrabble® franchise, “Yahtzee® With Buddies,” and many others. Across mobile, web, PC, and console, Scopely creates, develops, publishes, and live\-operates one of the most diversified and award\-winning portfolios in the games industry — bringing hundreds of millions of players together through a shared love of play. Founded in 2011, Scopely is powered by its exceptional team — including thousands of world\-class gamemakers around the globe, a distinctive tenet\-driven culture, and its proprietary technology platform, Playgami. Together, these strengths have fueled Scopely’s position as the \#1 mobile games company in the U.S. and \#2 globally, generating more than $10 billion in lifetime revenue. Whether building global sensations like “MONOPOLY GO!” from the ground up, or expanding through strategic acquisitions, including the FoxNext, GSN, and Niantic games businesses — Scopely consistently delivers experiences players love today and return to for years to come. Recognized multiple times as one of the "100 Most Influential Companies in the World" by TIME magazine and one of Fast Company's "World's Most Innovative Companies" and “Best Workplaces for Innovators,” Scopely believes that video games can be a force for good — creating meaningful connections, vibrant communities, and making life better through play. Scopely has global operations and partners across four continents in more than a dozen countries worldwide. For more information, visit: https://www.scopely.com/. Notice to Candidates: Scopely will never request payment or financial information during the application or hiring process. Please apply only through our official website and verify that all Talent Partner communications come from an email address ending in @scopely.com. Should you have any questions or encounter any fraudulent requests/emails/websites, please immediately contact recruiting@scopely.com. Our job applicant privacy policies are available here: California Privacy Notice and EEA/UK Privacy Notice. Employment at Scopely is based solely on a person's merit and qualifications. Scopely does not discriminate against any employee or applicant because of race, creed, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition (including breastfeeding), or any other basis protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law.

title: Process Systems Engineer
description: Objective (Across UAT and Go\-Live) * Proficiently utilise multiple systems applications to drive the operational performance of the site. * Order release management into the warehouse pick environment * Real time troubleshooting * Monitoring the Execution of the Wave Management process * Monitoring of replenishments * Adhere/ Chasing of pick sequencing/ routes * Responsible for the outbound process to ensure customer orders are dispatched * Reporting and closing all defects in the UAT steps MAIN DUTIES \& RESPONSIBILITIES Complete all UAT scenarios as assigned by line manager * Escalation of issues through documented escalation procedure * Identifying areas of improvement in day\-to\-day Operations QUALIFICATION, EXPERIENCE \& SKILLS REQUIRED * Minimum Honors Degree in an Engineering discipline * 3\- 4 years prior Process engineering experience * Experience in the Pharma/FMCG sectors an advantage * Lean six sigma qualification an advantage **Uniphar – Who we are** With a workforce of more than 3000 employees spread across Ireland, United Kingdom, Mainland Europe, MENA, and the USA, Uniphar is a trusted global partner to pharma and MedTech manufacturers, working to improve patient access to medicines around the world. Uniphar provides outsourced and specialized services to its clients, leveraging the strong relationships with 200\+ of the world’s best known pharmaco\-medical manufacturers across multiple geographies, enabled by our cutting\-edge digital technology and our highly expert teams. Uniphar is organised into three key divisions: Supply Chain Retail \& On Demand, Uniphar Med Tech and Uniphar Pharma. **Supply Chain \& Retail** Supply Chain Services provides both pre\-wholesale and wholesale distribution of pharmaceutical, healthcare and animal health products to pharmacies and hospital nationwide. The business supports a diverse customer base through the provision of strong service levels coupled with innovative commercial initiatives. In addition, the business provides services and supports that help independent community pharmacy to compete more effectively in an increasingly difficult environment and runs a network of Uniphar\-owned pharmacies under the Life, Allcare, McCauleys and Hickeys symbol brands. **Pharma – On Demand** The On\-Demand business is a specialist medical supply business that sources and distributes pharmaceuticals, medical equipment, and consumable supplies to healthcare professionals in over 160 countries worldwide. We have offices in Ireland, UK, Europe, USA and New Zealand. The company’s main divisions encompass everything from International Sales through Sales to NGO’s and Charities, Clinical Trials Supply, Imported (Unlicensed Medicines), Sexual Health Supplies, and the storage and distribution of medical products and devices for third parties**.** Click here to find out more: About Us \- Who we are \| Uniphar **Culture at Uniphar** Uniphar is a combination of many businesses each with its own identity, culture, values, and ways of working. Our overarching culture narrative is inclusive of each of these stores. Uniphar lives and breathes four key pillars which unpin and support our culture. We have a people first approach \- we do the right thing. We support entrepreneurial spirit where adaptability, commitment \& resilience is embodied in our way of working. We have a Common Purpose that connects our diversified businesses and people. Finally, Trust is at the heart of how we operate. Everything Uniphar does is enabled by our people and as we continue to grow domestically and internationally, we become more diverse. This diversity fuels our business and culture. **Sustainability** Sustainability is at the core of what we do and is deeply embedded in our business strategy. We want to contribute positively to the people and the world around us. Running our business in a sustainable way ensures that Uniphar will continue to prosper in the long\-term. We are focused on ensuring that each of the five pillars of our sustainability strategy are a fundamental part of our decision\-making process.

title: Product Designer (Hybrid)
description: ****About Us:**** A global SaaS leader for seamless digital document workflows, Nitro offers a suite of solutions for PDF, eSigning, identity verification and analytics supported by a best\-in\-class customer success and change management team. With more than 3 million licensed users and 13,000\+ business customers across 157 countries, we serve 67% of the Fortune 500\. ****How We Work:**** We aim to create an environment where talented individuals are empowered to excel. How we collaborate, innovate, and engage with one another is important to us. Our work is driven by 5 key principles: * One team, One mission Our collective dedication to Nitro's mission defines us. Together, we are building an environment where everyone feels like a valued part of something bigger than themselves. * Own it We take full ownership of our actions and decisions. We empower one another to lead with confidence, creativity, and a solutions\-focused mindset. * Accountable to our customers We are dedicated to our customers and take our commitments seriously. We do what we say we are going to do. * Excellence in execution Driven by passion and precision, we exemplify excellence in our delivery with innovative, top\-quality results. * Be bold, fail fast, learn faster We learn as we grow, dare to try, and bravely question. We are not chasing perfection but forever iterating towards it. These guiding values shape our approach to work, fostering a culture where everyone is inspired to contribute their best. **The Role** As a **Product Designer** at Nitro, you’ll play a key role in shaping complex product experiences that balance user needs, business outcomes, and technical feasibility. You’ll operate with a high degree of autonomy, partnering closely with Product, Engineering, and cross\-functional stakeholders to drive clarity, alignment, and quality across the product lifecycle. You’re a role model for Nitro’s ways of working \- bringing deep design and research expertise, sound judgment, and an inclusive, collaborative mindset. You’ll help elevate both the work and the people around you. ****What You’ll Be Doing**** **Practice** * Lead end\-to\-end product design across complex journeys and systems, delivering high\-quality, accessible, and performant experiences. * Independently plan, conduct, and synthesise user research using appropriate generative and evaluative methods. * Translate research insights into clear, scalable artefacts that inform design decisions and enable shared learning. * Use mapping, prototyping, and facilitation to explore ambiguity, connect user needs to business goals, and guide teams through discovery. * Visualise end\-to\-end journeys and service experiences, considering offline touchpoints, accessibility, and system constraints. * Apply strong judgment in balancing user value, business priorities, and technical feasibility. * Contribute as a subject matter expert, providing thoughtful critique and guidance across disciplines. **Culture** * Model Nitro’s standards, policies, and cultural norms through inclusive, ethical, and collaborative ways of working. * Foster strong cross\-functional relationships and actively bridge gaps between Product, Engineering, and other teams. * Proactively mentor and support less experienced designers and researchers, helping them grow their skills and confidence. * Strengthen the operational health of the team by improving rituals, workflows, tools, and templates. * Connect day\-to\-day work to broader company goals, helping others understand the “why” behind priorities. * Encourage experimentation, welcome failure as a learning opportunity, and promote continuous feedback and improvement. * Adapt to change with resilience and help teams navigate ambiguity with clarity and optimism. ****What We’re Looking For**** * Extensive experience (typically 4\+ years or equivalent) in product design, user experience, or related disciplines. * Deep fluency in human\-centred design and user research methods, with the ability to choose and adapt approaches based on context. * Proven ability to independently own complex problem spaces and deliver impactful, well\-reasoned solutions. * Strong facilitation, communication, and storytelling skills, with experience aligning diverse stakeholders. * Confidence working cross\-functionally and influencing outcomes without direct authority. * A creative, systems\-oriented problem\-solver who considers risks, trade\-offs, and multiple perspectives. * Experience mentoring others and contributing to team health and design maturity. * Comfort operating in changing environments and a willingness to continuously learn and evolve. **Why Nitro?** Along With Our Regular Benefits And Programs (including Health, Dental, Vision, And Retirement As Standard), We Are Also Very Proud To Offer a Few Additional Initiatives To Future Nitronauts: **Hybrid Work** Our team embraces the hybrid work model, appreciating its blend of flexibility and structure. We combine three days of in\-person collaboration at our global offices in Toronto, Dublin, Antwerp, Porto, and Melbourne with the convenience of two days of remote work each week. ****Benefits:**** Nitro provides all employees with a comprehensive benefits package that includes health insurance, dental and vision coverage, and wellness perks. We also offer pension/401k matching, along with many other country\-specific benefits. *Nitro strongly encourages applications from everyone regardless of race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status or disability status. We provide an accessible candidate experience and invite you to request any accommodations or adjustments throughout the interview process and beyond.*

title: Product Engineer
description: Fin is the AI Customer Agent company on a mission to help businesses provide perfect customer experiences. Our AI Agent Fin is the highest\-performing AI Customer Agent on the market today, enabling businesses to deliver impeccable, always\-on customer support across the customer journey – from service, to sales, to ecommerce. Powered by our own AI models, Fin resolves complex customer issues end\-to\-end across every channel, with minimal set\-up and integration. Fin can also be combined with our natively integrated Intercom help desk for one single system that is designed to meet the needs of modern day support teams. Founded in 2011, Fin became one of the fastest growing companies and remains one of the largest private software companies in the world with nearly 30,000 global businesses using our products to transform their customer support. Driven by our core values, we push boundaries, build with speed and intensity, and relentlessly deliver incredible value to our customers. What's the opportunity? At Fin, you will be a product engineer \- someone who solves real customer problems through a smart and efficient application of your technical knowledge and your tools. You’ll be part of one of our multidisciplinary product teams, where you will build both back\-end and front\-end systems, and work closely with designers, product managers, researchers, and data analysts. We’re facing many exciting scaling challenges and we’re building a robust platform where your expertise can be applied to areas such as building a beautiful messenger composer, rule matching, deliverability, security, app availability and machine learning, to name a few. What will I be doing? **As An Experienced Engineer You Will** * Develop technical plans and contribute to our technical architecture as we scale our products to serve tens of millions of people every day. * Write Ruby code, which knits together a lot of AWS, infrastructure, platform and SaaS technologies that form the core of Fin's backend infrastructure * Ship a change to production on your first day and a feature in your first week. That “day one” change is automatically deployed to production along with 100 other deployments (on average) each weekday. * Build using the best tools in the industry. We invest heavily in AI\-powered developer tools that remove friction and help you focus on solving meaningful problems. * Grow your team’s capacity by mentoring other engineers and interviewing candidates. This is a chance to be an integral part of building and growing a team. What skills do I need? * 2\+ years of industry experience in a software engineering role, preferably building a SaaS product. You can demonstrate significant impact that your work has had on the product and/or the team. * Deep knowledge of a high\-level programming language (for example, Ruby, Python, Javascript etc.) but it doesn’t need to be a language that we use here! Great people are effective and learn what we use quickly (or introduce us to better ways of working) * Experience collaborating directly with product teams and designers, and a proven track record of delivering value to customers or users. Engineers at Fin are pragmatists who work closely with others on cross\-disciplinary teams * Experience with Distributed systems What happens when I apply? First, apply so we can review your CV. As the next step, we will send you our take\-home test to work on in your own time. You can find more details on the questions and how we evaluate answers here. The following step will be a phone interview with one of our engineers which will focus on technical and culture contribution questions (this step is sometimes broken down into 2 interviews). The final step is a virtual onsite interview. **Benefits** We are a well treated bunch, with awesome benefits! If there’s something important to you that’s not on this list, talk to us! * Competitive salary and equity in a fast\-growing start\-up * We serve lunch every weekday, plus a variety of snack foods and a fully stocked kitchen * Regular compensation reviews \- we reward great work! * Pension scheme \& match up to 4% * Peace of mind with life assurance, as well as comprehensive health and dental insurance for you and your dependents * Flexible paid time off policy * Paid maternity leave, as well as 6 weeks paternity leave for fathers, to let you spend valuable time with your loved ones * If you’re cycling, we’ve got you covered on the Cycle\-to\-Work Scheme. With secure bike storage too * MacBooks are our standard, but we also offer Windows for certain roles when needed. **Policies** Fin has a hybrid working policy. We believe that working in person helps us stay connected, collaborate easier and create a great culture while still providing flexibility to work from home. We expect employees to be in the office at least three days per week. We have a radically open and accepting culture at Fin. We avoid spending time on divisive subjects to foster a safe and cohesive work environment for everyone. As an organization, our policy is to not advocate on behalf of the company or our employees on any social or political topics out of our internal or external communications. We respect personal opinion and expression on these topics on personal social platforms on personal time, and do not challenge or confront anyone for their views on non\-work related topics. Our goal is to focus on doing incredible work to achieve our goals and unite the company through our core values. Fin values diversity and is committed to a policy of Equal Employment Opportunity. Fin will not discriminate against an applicant or employee on the basis of race, color, religion, creed, national origin, ancestry, sex, gender, age, physical or mental disability, veteran or military status, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, marital status, or any other legally recognized protected basis under federal, state, or local law.

title: Product Security Engineer
description: **Company Overview** KKR is a leading global investment firm that offers alternative asset management as well as capital markets and insurance solutions. KKR aims to generate attractive investment returns by following a patient and disciplined investment approach, employing world\-class people, and supporting growth in its portfolio companies and communities. KKR sponsors investment funds that invest in private equity, credit and real assets and has strategic partners that manage hedge funds. KKR’s insurance subsidiaries offer retirement, life and reinsurance products under the management of Global Atlantic Financial Group. References to KKR’s investments may include the activities of its sponsored funds and insurance subsidiaries. **Position Summary** KKR is seeking an experienced Product Security Professional. This role offers exciting opportunities for growth and impact as KKR scales its business and continues to innovate. As a Security Analyst, you will be responsible for designing, implementing, and maintaining security measures across our environment specific to our internally developed applications and external facing applications. You must be proficient in troubleshooting, vulnerability management, cloud security, application security, and have a deep understanding of a wide range of systems, and be capable of leading other teams in these efforts. You will work closely with IT and other business units to ensure our security posture remains strong, aligned with industry best practices, and compliant with regulatory requirements. title: Production Operator
description: **Production Operator \| Global Pharmaceutical Manufacturing Leader** 📍 Carlow, Ireland 📅 11\-Month Fixed\-Term Contract 💼 Full\-Time, On\-Site We are currently seeking Production Operators to join a leading global pharmaceutical manufacturing facility based in Carlow. This is an excellent opportunity to gain experience within a highly regulated GMP environment, supporting the manufacture of life\-changing medicines while working with cutting\-edge technology and equipment. **The Opportunity** As a Production Operator, you will be part of a high\-performing Integrated Process Team (IPT), responsible for supporting manufacturing operations and ensuring the reliable supply of product while maintaining the highest standards of quality, compliance, and safety. Key Responsibilities * Operate and monitor production processes and associated equipment using MES, DCS, and PLC systems. * Support manufacturing activities across a range of production equipment, including sterilisation, washing, and filling systems. * Perform cleanroom cleaning, housekeeping, and materials management activities. * Manage equipment flow, inventory, labelling, and sterile equipment tracking through automated systems. * Assist with equipment maintenance, troubleshooting, and preventative maintenance activities where qualified. * Follow GMP procedures and ensure compliance with all quality, safety, and regulatory requirements. * Participate in investigations, root cause analysis, change controls, and continuous improvement initiatives. * Support Lean and Kaizen activities to improve operational efficiency. * Work collaboratively across departments to maintain a safe and compliant manufacturing environment. **What We're Looking For** Experience \& Skills * Previous experience in a manufacturing environment is desirable. * GMP or pharmaceutical industry experience would be highly advantageous. * Understanding of sterile manufacturing or aseptic processing is beneficial. * Strong troubleshooting and problem\-solving abilities. * Excellent attention to detail and commitment to quality. * Strong communication and teamwork skills. * Comfortable working with computerised systems and Microsoft Office applications. * Self\-motivated with strong organisational and time management skills. **Qualifications** * Leaving Certificate (or equivalent) required. * Apprenticeship, Certificate, Diploma, or relevant technical qualification preferred. * Previous experience in manufacturing, pharmaceutical, biotechnology, medical device, food production, or other regulated industries is highly desirable. **What You'll Gain** * Experience working within a world\-class pharmaceutical manufacturing environment. * Exposure to advanced manufacturing technologies and GMP operations. * Opportunity to contribute to the production of critical healthcare products. * Structured training and development within a highly skilled team. * Competitive hourly rate and benefits package. If you're looking to build your career within pharmaceutical manufacturing and want to join a company committed to quality, innovation, and patient impact, we'd love to hear from you. Apply today or contact us directly for a confidential discussion.

title: Professional Support Lawyer (Knowledge) — Projects, Infrastructure & Energy
description: **About Our Client** Our client is a leading, progressive law firm looking to welcome a Professional Support Lawyer (Knowledge) to their multi\-disciplinary Projects, Infrastructure \& Energy group. This is a unique, dynamic role that perfectly blends knowledge leadership with active legal practice. You will enjoy the variety of a strategic knowledge role while staying close to live client matters by carrying a manageable, reduced fee\-earning caseload. **The Role Overview** As a critical support hub for a busy and growing practice, you will provide technical know\-how, training, and support across a broad spectrum of areas, including: * Construction \& engineering * Public procurement * Energy \& natural resources * Planning \& environment * Housing \& ESG (including occasional support for the New Homes/Residential Development team) If you are a qualified solicitor who possesses a passion for technical accuracy, communicates complex legal points with clarity, and thrives on helping colleagues succeed, this role offers an exceptional platform for your career. **Key Responsibilities** * **Knowledge Management:** Develop, curate, and continuously improve the group's knowledge systems, document banks, precedents, and template advice across all core practice areas. * **Legal Analysis:** Monitor and analyze Irish and EU legislative, regulatory, and case\-law developments, translating them into practical guidance for fee earners. * **Training \& Professional Support:** Design and deliver internal training and CPD briefings for legal staff at all levels, while mentoring junior fee earners. * **Fee\-Earning Work:** Maintain a defined, manageable caseload of live matters alongside the wider team, billing in line with a reduced fee target that respects your dual responsibilities. * **Cross\-Departmental Collaboration:** Act as the first point of contact for technical queries, and collaborate with knowledge colleagues across the firm to share resources and best practices. * **Business Development:** Contribute to client\-facing articles, briefings, seminars, and thought\-leadership materials to help elevate the group's profile. **Person Specification** **Essential Criteria:** * Qualified Solicitor (Republic of Ireland) with relevant post\-qualification experience in one or more of the group's practice areas. * Strong technical research and analytical skills, with the ability to distil complex legal material into practical advice. * Exceptional drafting skills and an uncompromising eye for detail. * Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the confidence to deliver training. * Highly organized, self\-directed, and collaborative. **Desirable Criteria:** * Previous experience in a Professional Support Lawyer (PSL) or Knowledge role. * Familiarity with standard\-form construction contracts, public procurement procedures, or residential development. * Experience designing and delivering training or CPD programs. * Comfort utilizing legal technology, knowledge systems, and document automation tools. **What’s on Offer?** * **Broad Exposure:** Work across a multi\-disciplinary practice involved in significant Irish infrastructure, energy, and housing projects. * **The Best of Both Worlds:** A rare role that balances strategic knowledge leadership with continued hands\-on legal work. * **Modern Flexibility:** A supportive hybrid working model. Scope for Part Time. * **Reward:** A highly competitive remuneration and benefits package within a collegiate, growth\-oriented environment. If interested, apply directly or reach out to Caitriona Breaw @ Amicus Recruitment for a more detailed conversation.

title: Project Controls
description: Winthrop Technologies is Europe's only dedicated data centre delivery partner, offering full turnkey solutions from design through to construction and commissioning. Headquartered in Dublin, we deliver projects across Europe and have become the most trusted name in our sector. We are seeking a Junior Power BI Developer to join our growing data and digital function. This role is suited to a candidate with approximately 2\+ years' experience in business intelligence or data analytics who is looking to further develop their skills in a fast\-paced construction and engineering environment. You will work closely with teams across project controls, commercial, finance, and operations to develop insightful dashboards and reports that support data\-driven decision\-making across cutting\-edge data centre projects. **Key Responsibilities:** * Develop, maintain, and enhance Power BI dashboards and reports to support construction and data centre projects * Model, transform, and cleanse data using Power Query (M) and DAX * Combine data from multiple sources such as Excel, ERP systems, project management tools, and SQL databases * Collaborate with business stakeholders to gather requirements and translate them into clear, actionable visual reports * Support data quality, consistency, and governance across reporting outputs * Assist with automation of recurring and manual reporting processes * Maintain documentation for dashboards, datasets, and reporting processes * Apply and promote best practices in BI development and data visualisation **Requirements** **Requirements:** * 2\+ years' experience in a Power BI Developer, Data Analyst, or BI\-related role * Degree in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Business Analytics, or a related discipline * Strong hands\-on experience with Power BI in a professional environment * Solid understanding of: Data modelling concepts, DAX and Power Query (M), SQL or other data querying tools (desirable) * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills with high attention to detail * Ability to communicate effectively with both technical and non\-technical stakeholders * Interest in working within construction, engineering, or data centre infrastructure projects * Experience with integrating AI (Copilot) with Power BI semantic models for Q\&A across reporting **Benefits** * Opportunity to work on Europe's largest and most advanced data centre projects * Career growth and professional development in a fast\-expanding sector * Competitive salary package * Supportive, dynamic, and collaborative team culture

title: Project Delivery Engineer
description: Elusav Recruitment have an excellent opportunity for a Projects Delivery Engineer for a leading Engineering and Technical Services Contractor in the water and wastewater treatment space. They are looking for a Projects Delivery Engineer who can support and manage a diverse range of design projects, both greenfield and brownfield, as well as support preconstruction/proposal activities. This role sits within the companies contracting division, but they also operate large managed services contracts for Ireland largest WWTP facilities, where majority of the projects are delivered. The Projects Delivery Engineer will report to the Senior Projects managers and support the delivery of critical projects up to the value of €40 Million. Excellent opportunity to join an industry leader with a significant footprint in the wastewater treatment space in Ireland. Long term local opportunity, and also strong remuneration package. **Responsibilities:** * Production of documentation, including reports, drawings, calculations, specifications, tender documentation, and particular specifications. * Production of project budgets and resource\-loaded detailed work programmes. * Co\-ordinating with PSCS, PSDP, Client, Operations and other stakeholders. * Providing technical advice and guidance. * Providing input to bids and project managing appropriate projects. * Supervision \& management of contractors. * Responding to Client requests for information. * Preparation and/or review RAMS. * Identify interfaces with operations, impacts, consequences, work\-arounds and plan, execute operations dependent tasks within the suitable window. * Support maintenance by managing SLAs and contractors. * Identify and prioritise capital maintenance projects based on equipment/process condition and criticality. **Requirements:** * Good knowledge of techniques for planning, monitoring and controlling projects. * Ability to risk assess equipment and criticality * Have experience in design at all levels, from feasibility to a "for construction" level. * Have considerable technical knowledge of all aspects of design as it impacts technical, budgetary and programme issues. * PRINCE2 or PMP qualification or experience in a PRINCE2/PMP structured project is preferred. **Contact:** If you would like to learn more about the vacancy, apply now, or contact Brendan Mullins at bmullins@elusav.com or \+353 61 527 150\.

title: Project P6 Scheduler – Project Management Office (PMO)
description: **Project P6 Scheduler** **Location:** Dublin, Cork, Galway. **Join Us In Shaping The Future Of Project Delivery** Join a leading team where your planning expertise will help shape the successful delivery of complex engineering and infrastructure projects. This is an excellent opportunity for an experienced Primavera P6 Scheduler to play a key role within a Project Management Office (PMO), supporting project managers and contributing directly to improved schedule performance, risk management, and project outcomes. **Purpose \& Scope Of The Role** We are seeking a Primavera P6 Scheduler to join the Project Management Office (PMO) and support Project Managers across a range of business sectors. The successful candidate will be responsible for assisting with the efficient delivery of projects and programmes, with particular focus on schedule management, risk, resourcing, and cost control. The role involves developing, maintaining, and analysing project schedules for multi\-disciplinary design projects from concept through to construction and handover. It also includes review and integration of contractor and supplier programmes into master project schedules, supporting reporting requirements, and helping to identify and implement corrective actions where required. **Your Impact In This Position** In this role, you will make a meaningful contribution to the successful delivery of projects by ensuring schedules are accurate, robust, and aligned with project objectives. You will help Project Managers maintain control of time, cost, and resources, while also supporting risk planning and analysis. Your work will directly improve visibility of project performance, strengthen decision\-making, and support the timely delivery of complex programmes. You will also contribute to continuous improvement by analysing project trends, capturing lessons learned, and supporting planning training and workshops. **Core Capabilities** **Planning \& Scheduling** Strong knowledge of project planning and scheduling principles, methods, and tools. Ability to develop, coordinate, track, and manage project schedules, identify critical issues, and support schedule performance across individual and portfolio\-level projects. **Attention to Detail** High level of accuracy in reviewing, interrogating, and maintaining schedules, cost data, and reporting outputs. Able to spot issues early and ensure information is reliable and actionable. **Communication \& Stakeholder Engagement** Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to work effectively with Project Managers, Clients, Contractors, Consultants, and internal stakeholders in a collaborative environment. **Problem Solving \& Initiative** Proactive approach to identifying schedule risks and issues, recommending corrective actions, and supporting timely resolution of complex planning challenges. **Teamwork \& Self\-Management** Works well as part of a multi\-disciplinary team, with strong organisational skills, good time management, and the ability to self\-motivate and deliver to deadlines. **Qualifications \& Experience** * Relevant degree or higher diploma in project management, civil engineering, or a related discipline. * Minimum 5\+ years’ experience in project planning, scheduling, and control across road and rail projects in Ireland, varying in size and complexity. * Experience working in an engineering design consultancy in the UK or Ireland as a Primavera P6 Scheduler. Experience should include logic development, developing and maintaining baselines, critical path mapping / analysis, cost and resource\-loading and adjustments including levelling, reporting metrics including earned value, e.g. SPI (Schedule Performance Index) and resource / cashflow profiling. * Proven knowledge of programme, risk, and resource management across engineering projects. * Experience developing Basis of Schedule documentation to support project schedules. * Strong report writing skills and the ability to present schedule information clearly and professionally. * Experience assisting with planning training and facilitating planning workshops is beneficial. * Experience with quantitative risk analysis techniques is advantageous. * Strong technical experience in delivery of projects within engineering consultancies and/or related construction projects. * Demonstrated experience working within accredited Integrated Management Systems (IMS) \& Standards, including quality, data security, and risk. * Proficient in MS Office Suite, particularly MS Excel and MS PowerPoint. **Working Environment** This role is based in Ireland and will involve working closely with Project Managers and multi\-disciplinary teams across a range of sectors. The position requires collaboration with internal stakeholders, Clients, Consultants, and Contractors. Flexibility to support project needs across different locations and working arrangements may be required, depending on project demands. Hybrid, remote, or flexible working arrangements can be incorporated where business and project requirements allow. **Why join Tetra Tech?** Tetra Tech is a leading provider of consulting and engineering services working across the full project lifecycle worldwide. We have a wide range of expertise across our teams, providing a global support network with a personalised approach to client relationships to better understand where we can add value. We provide a collaborative environment that supports individual performance, innovation, and creativity. W e support public and private sector clients on local, national, and international projects to deliver sustainable and resilient solutions. Our operations in the UK, Ireland, and the Netherlands include more than 6,000 employees who are Leading with Science® to solve our clients’ most complex problems. In alignment with the Equality Act 2010, we will make reasonable adjustments to support candidates and employees requiring additional arrangements. This could include adaptations to work schedules, training approaches, or the physical workspace. Please inform us if you need any accommodations during the recruitment process or in your day\-to\-day role. **Recruitment Agencies** We have a Preferred Supplier List of trusted partners to assist us when required and do not acknowledge any speculative CVs or unsolicited candidate introductions from agencies not on this list and any submitted will be taken as a gift. Please refer to the careers page on our website if you require any further information.

title: Quality Control Inspector
description: **Company Description** Camera Inspection Services Ltd (CIS) is a leading provider of Non\-Destructive Testing (NDT) solutions in Ireland, specializing in evaluating the integrity of buildings, assets, and systems. Using advanced technologies such as thermal imaging, moisture testing, and UAV aerial surveys, CIS helps clients identify hidden issues and develop effective maintenance plans. The company ensures unbiased reporting as it operates independently without affiliations to repair or maintenance companies. CIS delivers high\-quality inspection reports, including detailed imagery, to support precise problem identification. Based in Dublin, CIS is committed to adapting its services to meet unique client needs. **Role Description** This is a short term role for a Quality Control Inspector, with projects in Dublin, Cork, Sligo \& Laois The Quality Control Inspector will be responsible for conducting asset and property inspections using advanced NDT techniques. Key responsibilities include performing site inspections, preparing detailed reports, identifying defects, and ensuring compliance with quality standards. The role requires collaboration with internal teams and clients to provide actionable insights and recommendations. **Desired Qualifications** * Proficiency in Quality Control and Quality Assurance processes * Experience in Inspection and Quality Management in a related field * Strong Communication skills to document findings and interact with clients and teams * Ability to operate NDT equipment, such as thermal imaging and other diagnostic tools * Attention to detail and problem\-solving skills * Working knowledge of safety standards and regulations is an advantage * Relevant certifications in NDT or quality\-related disciplines are preferred * Valid driver’s license and willingness to travel for site visits Delivering exceptional quality and innovation in every project. Exciting Opportunities across; Civil Engineering \| Construction Technology \| Architecture / Architectural Technology \| Programme Immediate Start Duration Summer 2026 Locations Dublin \| Cork \| Sligo \| Laois Remuneration Negotiable depending on experience Sectors Industrial, Commercial, Civil Infrastructure \& Residential About: Camera Inspection Services provide Technical Services to the Industrial, Commercial, Civil Infrastructure, and Residential sectors across Ireland and the United Kingdom. With a commitment to delivering exceptional quality and innovation in every project, Camera Inspection Services work at the forefront of structural investigation and remediation. www.camerainspectionservices.ie The Opportunity Camera Inspection Services is inviting applications from motivated individuals to fill positions across our Dublin, Cork, and Sligo offices. This is an excellent opportunity to gain hands\-on, practical experience on live remediation and technical services projects alongside our experienced engineering team. Placement students will gain direct experience in: •Structural remediation projects across multiple sectors •Site investigation techniques and condition assessment •Project documentation, reporting and verification •Technical services delivery in a professional environment Eligible Disciplines We welcome applications from candidates with experience in: •Civil Engineering •Construction Technology •Architecture or Architectural Technology •Any related built environment or engineering discipline Key Requirements •Experience in the Construction industry•Competent IT skills, including proficiency with standard office and technical software•Excellent command of the English language — both written and spoken •Strong organisational skills, attention to detail and ability to manage tasks effectively •Clear and confident communication skills •Eager to learn, actively participate and make a meaningful contribution to the team What We Offer •Competitive remuneration paid weekly on an hourly basis•Practical, real\-world experience on live structural remediation projects•Mentorship and guidance from qualified engineers and technical specialists•Opportunities in Dublin, Cork and Sligo to suit your location•A supportive, professional and collaborative working environment•Valuable industry exposure to strengthen your experience and professional profile How to Apply To apply, please send your CV and a cover letter detailing any relevant education, and any prior experience to: dan@camerainspectionservices.ie Subject line: Summer 2026 — \[Your Name] — \[Location Preference] Structuretek is an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from all qualified candidates. www.camerainspectionservices.ie.ie

title: Receptionist - Front Of House - Baggot Street, D2
description: ### **Who We Are** Truly Dental is a modern dental company operating across Ireland, the UK, and the UAE, dedicated to high\-tech, compassionate, quality dental care. On our Baggot Street, Dublin 2 clinic**,** we deliver cutting\-edge dentistry in a welcoming environment. ### **Our Impact \- Fast Facts** * Flagship network covering Ireland, the UK \& UAE. * 30\+ treatment rooms at a major Dublin site (indicative of the network's capacity and scale). * On\-site lab \& digital radiography/CBCT enabling timely diagnostics and treatment. * Extended opening hours (Mon\-Sun) to maximise patient access in city\-centre and regional sites. ### **Purpose of the Vacancy** As a Front of House Team Member at Baggot Street, Dublin 2, you will be the welcoming first point of contact for patients and play a key role in delivering five\-star service, supporting appointment flow, patient check\-in/out, phone and inbox management, and helping ensure that clinical teams can deliver outstanding care. ### **Key Responsibilities** * Warmly greet, welcome, and check in/out patients; provide clear, empathetic information at all times. * Manage a multiline phone system and highvolume inbox/queries; prioritise between in\-person and remote patient needs. * Schedule, reschedule, and confirm appointments; coordinate with clinical teams to optimise diaries. * Prepare patient charts and documentation for dental staff; update and maintain accurate patient records. * Generate billing statements, process payments, reconcile daily cash takings, and handle deposits / clerical handoff as required. * Operate clinic software (training provided; network uses EXACT practice management system) and adhere to data\-protection protocols. * Support changeover periods and occasional weekend shifts as required; collaborate with the team to maintain service across full opening hours. * Uphold Truly's hospitality standards and contribute actively to a friendly, cooperative team culture. ### **Requirements \- Competencies \& Skills** **Education** * Secondary education required; vocational/admin qualification is an advantage. **Experience** * Minimum 1\-2 years in front\-of\-house or reception roles; dental or hospital reception experience strongly preferred. * Proven success in high\-volume, customer\-facing environments. **Knowledge \& Skills** * Experience with multiline phone handling, appointment scheduling, billing, and cash reconciliation. * Proficient or willing to learn clinic practice management systems (Dentally experience is a plus). * Strong multitasking, organisation, and attention to detail in a busy setting. * Excellent spoken and written English; able to communicate clearly and empathetically with patients from diverse backgrounds. * Comfortable working closely with clinicians and senior specialists; professional discretion and awareness of GDPR/data protection requirements. **Core Competencies** * Patient\-centric service \& empathy * Stress tolerance \& prioritisation in a high\-volume environment * Teamwork \& cross\-functional coordination * Accountability \& reliability (punctuality, accuracy of cash/records) * Learning agility (adapting to systems \& processes) ### **What We Offer** * Attractive part\-time terms and salary for the right candidate; overtime paid as applicable. * Career progression opportunities: admin support, training, and guidance in a fast\-growing dental network. * A fun, modern working environment with up\-to\-date dental software and processes. * Training on Dentally system for the successful candidate, supporting your growth in the role. * Flexibility and variety: part\-time hours, weekend shifts, opportunity to support across clinic flow and service. ### **Important Details Before Applying** * Full\-time role based at Baggot Street, Dublin 2 \- full flexibility required, including weekends. * Shift pattern will involve rotation across early/late appointments to meet clinic hours; exact schedule to be agreed.

title: Recruiting Coordinator - 6 Month Contract
description: ****🚀 Join the Future of Commerce with Whatnot!**** Whatnot is the largest livestream shopping platform in North America and Europe to buy, sell, and discover the things you love. Whether it's trading cards, fashion, electronics, or live plants, our sellers are building real businesses across hundreds of categories. We're building live commerce at a scale that's never been done in the West, and there's no playbook to copy. The people here are shaping how an entirely new industry develops. As a remote co\-located team, we're inspired by our values and anchored in hubs across the US, UK, Ireland, Poland, Germany, and Australia. We move fast, stay close to our users, and focus on the work that drives the most impact. We're one of the fastest growing marketplaces and were recently named the \#1 Best Startup Employer in America by Forbes. Check out the latest Whatnot updates on our news and engineering blogs and join us as we enable anyone to turn their passion into a business and bring people together through commerce. ****💻 Role**** * Own scheduling logistics across all teams and international time zones — ensuring interviews are confirmed accurately and both candidates and interviewers have all necessary details. * Collaborate closely with recruiters and hiring managers to manage complex scheduling, proactively flag conflicts, and communicate updates clearly and promptly. * Leverage Ashby for scheduling, tracking, and analytics — automating workflows where possible to improve efficiency and maintain a consistent candidate experience. * Track and report recruiting metrics to keep the hiring team informed and identify opportunities to optimize the recruiting process. * Drive recruiting enablement, including maintaining documentation, helping with trainings, and fielding ad hoc requests. * Build strong partnerships with recruiters and hiring managers to anticipate scheduling or process challenges and resolve them before they become blockers. * Contribute to team projects and initiatives, taking ownership and driving them to completion with minimal supervision. **Team members in this role are required to be within commuting distance of our Dublin, Ireland hub and are open to in\-office work.** ****👋 You**** People who do well at Whatnot tend to be comfortable figuring things out as they go, biased toward action, and genuinely curious about what they're building. They care more about outcomes than credit and stay close to the product and the people using it. * Experienced: You have at least 1 year of experience as a Recruiting Coordinator, ideally in a fast\-paced or startup environment. * Highly organized: You balance multiple priorities with precision and always follow through on commitments. * Proactive and dependable: You anticipate needs, communicate effectively, and take initiative rather than waiting for direction. * Candidate\-obsessed: You care deeply about creating a smooth, human experience for every candidate and hiring partner. * Tech\-savvy: You’re comfortable using recruiting tools like Ashby — and you’re curious and resourceful when learning new systems. * Collaborative: You build trust across teams and enjoy being a helpful, reliable partner. * Growth\-minded: You welcome feedback, look for ways to improve, and lean into action to make an impact. ****💛 EOE**** Whatnot is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We value diversity, and we do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, parental status, disability status, or any other status protected by local law. We believe that our work is better and our company culture is improved when we encourage, support, and respect the different skills and experiences represented within our workforce. Compensation Range: €59K \- €65K

title: Recruitment Specialist (Accepting All Backgrounds)

But growth alone isn’t what defines us. We’re building something different. A business where performance drives progression. Where ambition is matched with real opportunity. People are at the core of everything we do. We invest heavily in training, mentorship, and internal progression \- giving you the tools and support to grow fast and succeed. **What makes Access Healthcare different?** From day one, you’ll be trusted with real responsibility, supported by experienced leaders, and encouraged to take ownership of your growth. This is a place for ambitious individuals who want to make an impact and build a long\-term career. **🌟 A Day in the Life – 6 Months In 🌟** No two days are the same. Mornings start with coffee and a quick team huddle to set goals, then it’s straight into **live search assignments, writing standout job ads, and keeping multiple processes moving at pace** . The rest of the day is about **people, momentum, and results** \- meeting ambitious candidates, partnering with clients, prepping talent for interviews, and celebrating wins as deals come together. Along the way, you’ll level up your skills through **Certified Recruitment Consultant (CRC) Training** , before wrapping up with a high\-energy team round\-up full of laughs, support, and shared wins. **What’s in it for You?** * **Expert mentorship \& training** – Learn from the best in the market and skyrocket your career. * **Long\-term growth** – We invest in you from day one, aiming to help you reach senior and leadership roles. * **Merit\-based success** – Your effort drives your progress. The harder you work, the faster you climb. * **Fast promotions** – Most consultants get promoted in **6 months** , top performers in just 3\. How fast do you want to rise? * **Friday fun** – Kick back with a fully stocked beer fridge and after\-work bonding with your team. **Our Social Life** It’s not all work. Our dedicated social committee organises events all year: outdoor adventures, quizzes, nights out, welness activities and more! **Salary \& Earning Potential** We offer a base salary plus uncapped commissions \- your earning potential is unlimited: * Average performers earn around €45k in year one (base \+ commission) * Year two typically rises to €75k For more information, email your CV at francisca.simoes@accesshealthcare.ie No prior recruitment experience is required \- just the motivation to learn and succeed.

title: Sales Development Representative
description: Clio is the global leader in legal AI technology, empowering legal professionals and law firms of every size to work smarter, faster, and more securely.
